Digitized by Google
Original from
UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

FOREWORD
The 1954 General Assembly imposed upon the Legislative
Research Commission the duty of furnishing the printer correct
copy of bills and resolutions for inclusion in the Session Acts .
Section 57.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes stipulates that
bound copies of Session Acts be delivered and distributed within
sixty days after the end of a regular session .
This time limit is based on sound reasons . The majority of
legislation enacted by any session of the General Assembly
becomes effective as law ninety days after adjournment . Re-
sponsible public officers and other interested citizens need to have
a copy of the Session Acts prior to this effective date . Until the
publication of the Session Acts in 1956 the most prompt delivery
had been some 120 days following the close of the session .
In the preparation of this volume of the 1958 Session Acts
the Secretary of State has furnished access to copy of filed en-
rolled bills and resolutions . The staff of the Research Division of
the Legislative Research Commission compared the prepared page
proofs with the enrolled bills . In order to expedite furnishing
copy to the printer , bills and resolutions are inserted by chapters
according to the order in which they received gubernatorial action
and were filed in the office of the Secretary of State . Miss Dorothy
Kavanaugh and Mr. Joseph A. LaPage on the research staff of
the Commission have prepared this volume under the supervision
of James T. Fleming , Director of Research .
Frankfort , Kentucky
May 1, 1958
HARRY LEE WATERFIELD
Chairman
Legislative Research Commission

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE REGULAR SESSION OF THE GENERAL ASSEM-
BLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT , KEN-
TUCKY , ON TUESDAY , JANUARY THE SEVENTH , 1958 , AND ENDED
FRIDAY , MARCH THE TWENTY -FIRST, 1958 .
CHAPTER 1
(H. R. 22 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION making an appropriation to the Legislative Re-
search Commission for special expenses incurred in connection with
sessions of the General Assembly and declaring an emergency .
WHEREAS , additional personnel are necessary to expedite properly the
business of the Senate and the House of Representatives ; and
WHEREAS , the present appropriation of the Legislative Research Com-
mission is insufficient to provide adequate clerical services to the
General Assembly ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
That there is appropriated out of the General Expenditure
Fund to the Legislative Research Commission the sum of one
thousand and five hundred dollars per calendar day during all
regular and special sessions of the General Assembly . This ap-
propriation shall be available for expenditure during the 1957-58
fiscal year.
WHEREAS , the General Assembly is now in session and it
is necessary that these funds be available immediately , an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this resolution shall become effec-
tive upon its passage and approval by the Governor , and shall
be retroactive to include payment of compensation to such per-
sonnel from and including January 7 , 1958 .
CHAPTER 2
(H. B. 1)
Approved February 6 , 1958
AN ACT relating to appropriations for the operation , maintenance , sup-
port and functioning of the Government of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and its various officers , departments , boards , institutions ,
subdivisions , agencies and other state supported activities .

2 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
PART ONE
There is appropriated out of the General Expenditure Fund ,
including any unappropriated surplus to the General Expendi-
ture Fund as of June 30, 1958 , for the fiscal year beginning
July 1 , 1958 , and ending June 30 , 1959 , and for the fiscal year
beginning July 1 , 1959, and ending June 30, 1960 , the follow-
ing sums for the following officers , departments , boards , com-
missions , institutions , and subdivisions of the state government ,
and any and all other agencies of the state government of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky subject to the provisions of Chap-
ters 12 , 42 , and 45 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , for which
a specific appropriation is in Part One listed as follows :

The State Board of Education shall not approve any work-
ing budget or salary schedule for local boards of education for
the school years 1958-59 and 1959-60 in which the total amount
budgeted for teachers ' salaries is not equal to or greater than
the amount to be included in the foundation program for a
district for teachers ' salaries as determined under KRS 157.390 .
No working budget or salary schedule shall be approved in
which the average salary paid any rank of teacher is not at
least equivalent to the allotment for that rank and in which
any teacher is paid less than ninety per cent of the foundation
program allotment for that rank .

18 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to any
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall not allot
school district more basic classroom units than the num-
ber of teachers employed by that school district for basic class-
room units . No basic classroom units shall be allotted which
increase in 1958-59 or 1959-60 the differential between the
pupil-teacher ratio of the secondary grades (9-12 ) and the
elementary grades ( 1-8 ) in 1957-58 . No basic classroom units
shall be approved which provide in a given building or school
a lower pupil-teacher ratio than twenty -seven in average daily
attendance to one or the pupil-teacher ratio which obtained in
1957-58 , whichever is lower .
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall allot class-
room units for superintendents , principals , their certified assist-
ants and special instructional personnel as provided in KRS
157.360 , subsection 6 , only for personnel who are qualified for
their respective positions under provisions of law and regulations
of the State Board of Education and who maintain programs
approved under regulations of the State Board of Education .
The number of units allotted by the Superintendent of
Public Instruction for vocational education and the education
of exceptional children shall not exceed the following :
Vocational Agriculture
Vocational Home Economics
Trades and Industries
Distributive Occupations

158 180Exceptional Children
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall not allot a
full classroom unit for any one -teacher school with fewer
than 25 pupils in average daily attendance unless it has been
determined on the basis of a survey that no facilities are avail-
able to which these pupils could be transported under regu-
lations of the State Board of Education and no full classroom
units shall be allotted for any one -teacher school which has
fewer than 12 pupils in average daily attendance .
Facilities that are used to house school children shall be
approved by the Superintendent of Public Instruction under
KRS 162.060 and 162.160 . No alterations or additions of old
buildings shall be made without prior approval of the Super-
intendent of Public Instruction . No space shall be rented or

CHAPTER 2 19
purchased and converted to classroom use without prior ap-
proval of the Superintendent of Public Instruction . The State
Board of Education may adopt regulations for the approval on
an annual basis of temporary facilities for the housing of school
children but these regulations shall not permit the division of
any present room by partitions of nonstructural material . No
classroom units shall be allotted for a second teacher in the
same classroom . No facilities shall be approved for classroom
use which are detrimental to the health of or dangerous to the
safety of school children .
If a school district operates any school or grade of a school
for a term of less than nine scholastic months or for a school
day of less than the minimum required by KRS 158.060 , the
average daily attendance of such school shall be reduced pro-
portionately by the Superintendent of Public Instruction in
determining the allotment of classroom units provided for under
KRS 157.360 , subsection 2 , except as provided for by regulations
of the State Board of Education .
If at the time of the tentative allotment of school funds
under KRS 157.310 through 157.440 , it shall be determined that
insufficient revenue is available to finance in full the foun-
dation program including a provision that no district receive
less than $ 80 per child in average daily attendance and no dis-
trict receive less total state aid than it received from the Per
Capita Fund in 1955-56 , the Superintendent of Public Instruc-
tion shall not allot any classroom units under KRS 157.360 ,
subsection 3. So much of the public school foundation program
fund, which would not be distributed during 1958-59 due to
the failure of one or more participating districts to meet the
full requirements , shall be held in account for distribution the
following year among districts which meet requirements for
participation .
Beginning August 1 of each year and on the first of each
month thereafter for seven successive months , one -twelfth of
each district's share of the foundation program fund less capital
outlay shall be distributed . On or before March 1 of each year
the Superintendent of Public Instruction shall determine the
exact amount of the public school foundation program fund to
which each district is entitled , and the remainder of the amount
due each district for the year less capital outlay shall be dis-

for three successive months thereafter . Capital outlay allot-
ments may be made twice a year .

III . ADMINISTRATION AND EFFECT
All funds appropriated to the Department of Highways
by section I of Part Two of this Act ( Highway Department
Generally ) shall be allocated equitably by the Commissioner
of Highways , with the approval of the Commissioner of Fi-
nance , between the various activities and functions of the De-
partment of Highways .
The appropriations made by this Act are subject to the pro-
visions of Chapters 12 , 42 , and 45 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes . Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal any ap-
propriation for the fiscal year to the State Road Fund ending
June 30, 1958 .
PART THREE

institutions , agencies and/or subdivisions of the state govern-

activities ) and rentals, admittances , sales , licenses collected by

received by any of the following agencies of the state govern-
ment, except as otherwise specifically provided , for the use and
benefit of each of the below -named agencies of the state gov-
ernment for the purposes for which each fund is authorized .

of the state government listed herein shall be placed to the
credit of a Revolving or Trust and Agency Fund out of which
shall be established separate Revolving or Trust and Agency
Fund Accounts for the use and benefit of these agencies of the
state government for purposes for which each fund is authorized .

of this Appropriation Act , the excess is appropriated to the credit
of said account for the fiscal year in which the excess occurs ;

PART FOUR
GENERAL PROVISION
1. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal any
appropriation for the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1958 , and noth-
ing in this Act is to be construed to amend or alter the provi-
sions of Chapter 42 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes pertain-
ing to the duties and powers of the Commissioner of Finance .
All questions that arise in interpreting any appropriation in
this Act as to the purpose for which the same may be expended
shall be decided by the Commissioner of Finance in accordance
with the detailed estimates embodied in the budget report as
considered by the General Assembly , and the decision of the
Commissioner of Finance shall be final and conclusive .

CHAPTER 2 41
2. Whereas , the finances of the Commonwealth can not
be ascertained at this time , and since the actual needs of each
officer , department , board , commission , institution , or subdi-
vision of the state government can only be anticipated and not
finally determined by the Legislature , and since the total amount
of revenue accruing to the General Expenditure Fund from which
these appropriations are made can only be anticipated through
existing laws and those that may hereafter be enacted , it is the
purpose and intention of this Act that the expenditures for each
fiscal year under Part I in this Act shall not exceed the total
revenues anticipated to accrue to the General Expenditure Fund
for the same fiscal year . As the conditions can not be fully de-
termined until after the adjournment of the 1958 General As-
sembly, and as the needs of each officer , department , board ,
commission , institution , or subdivision of the state government
and the total revenues accruing to the General Fund can not
be fully determined at this time , it is the intention and the pur-
pose of this General Assembly that the Commissioner of Fi-
nance , with the advice of the Commissioner of Revenue and
the approval of the Governor of the Commonwealth , is au-
thorized , empowered and directed to ascertain and determine
the needs of each officer , department , board , commission , in-
stitution or subdivision of the state government , and to ascer-
tain the total revenue anticipated to accrue to the General
Expenditure Fund of the Commonwealth , and upon the ascer-
tainment and determination of said facts , he , the Commissioner
of Finance , in the manner set forth and as provided by general
law , is authorized , empowered and directed by the General
Assembly to prevent an overdraft or deficit in any fiscal year
for which appropriations are herein made , by equitably reducing ,
according to need, without discrimination , the appropriations
herein made in Part I to any officer , department , board , com-
mission , institution , or subdivision of the state government ; pro-
vided that the power vested shall not permit any reduction of the
appropriation of any officer , department , board , commission , in-
stitution , or subdivision of the state government that will actually
impair the necessary constitutional functions of any agency whose
operations and functions are determined to be necessary consti-
tutional functions of government .

42 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
3. In the event receipts to the General Expenditure Fund
from current revenues for the fiscal year 1957-58 exceed One
Hundred Thirty Million Dollars ( $ 130,000,000 ) and for the fis-
cal year 1958-59 exceed One Hundred Thirty - One Million Dol-
lars ( $ 131,000,000 ) , such excesses are appropriated to the De-
partment of Finance to be used for capital construction in
accordance with the provisions of Chapter 56 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes.
4. In enacting this Budget Appropriation Law , it is the
deliberate intention of the General Assembly to enact each sec-
tion , and each subsection thereof , as a separate and/or specific
appropriation and law, and if any section or any subsection
thereof shall be held invalid , or unconstitutional , the decision
of the courts shall not affect or impair any of the remaining sec-
tions , subsections , or provisions contained herein .
5. All continuing appropriations in existence at the time
this Act takes effect are discontinued and repealed by this Act,
except as provided by Chapters 12 , 42 , 45 , 56 , and 177 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes . All laws and parts of laws in con-
flict with any of the provisions of the above enactments , to the
extent of such conflict , are repealed .
CHAPTER 3
(H. B. 2 )
AN ACT relating to income taxes .
Approved February 11 , 1958
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 141.020 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) With respect to taxable years beginning after Decem-
ber 31 , 1957 , an annual tax , consisting of a normal tax and a
surtax , shall be paid for each taxable year by every resident in-
dividual of this state , upon his entire net income as defined in
this chapter . Normal tax shall be determined by applying the
rates in subsection (2) to net income and subtracting allowable
tax credits provided in subsection (3) . Surtax shall be determined
according to the rates in subsection (4) . The annual tax due
shall be the sum of the normal tax and the surtax .

CHAPTER 3 43
(2) The following rates shall be applied to net income :
(a) Two percent of the amount of net income not exceeding
three thousand dollars ;
(b) Three percent of the amount of net income in excess
of three thousand dollars but not in excess of four thousand
dollars ;
(c) Four percent of the amount of net income in excess of
four thousand dollars but not in excess of five thousand dollars ;
(d) Five percent of the amount of net income in excess
of five thousand dollars but not in excess of eight thousand
dollars ;
(e) Six percent of the amount of net income in excess of
eight thousand dollars .
(3) The following tax credits , when applicable , shall be
deducted from the result obtained under subsection (2) to arrive
at normal tax.
(a) Thirteen dollars for an unmarried individual .
(b) Thirteen dollars for a married individual filing a sep-
arate return and an additional thirteen dollars for the spouse
of taxpayer if a separate return is made by the taxpayer and if
the spouse , for the calendar year in which the taxable year of
the taxpayer begins , has no gross income and is not the de-
pendent of another taxpayer ; or twenty -six dollars for married
persons filing a joint return , provided neither spouse is the de-
pendent of another taxpayer . The determination of marital status
for the purpose of this section shall be made in the manner pre-
scribed in section 153 of the Internal Revenue Code .
(c) Thirteen dollars credit for each dependent whose gross
income for the calendar year in which the taxable year of the
taxpayer begins is less than six hundred dollars , or who is a child ,
as defined in section 151 (e) (3 ) of the Internal Revenue Code ,
of the taxpayer and who (i) has not attained the age of 19 at the
close of the calendar year in which the taxable year of the tax-
payer begins , or (ii ) is a student . No exemption shall be allowed
for any dependent who has made a joint return with his spouse .
(d) An additional thirteen dollars credit if the taxpayer

44 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
has attained the age of sixty-five before the close of the taxable
year .
(e) An additional thirteen dollars credit for taxpayer's
spouse if a separate return is made by the taxpayer and if the
taxpayer's spouse has attained the age of sixty-five before the
close of the taxable year , and , for the calendar year in which
the taxable year of the taxpayer begins , has no gross income
and is not the dependent of another taxpayer .
(f) An additional thirteen dollars credit if the taxpayer is
blind at the close of the taxable year .
(g) An additional thirteen dollars credit for taxpayer's
spouse if a separate return is made by the taxpayer and if the
taxpayer's spouse is blind, and , for the calendar year in which
the taxable year of the taxpayer begins , has no gross income
and is not the dependent of another taxpayer .
(h) In the case of nonresidents , the tax credits allowable
under this subsection shall be that proportion of those per-
mitted therein that is represented by the ratio of the taxpayer's
net income in this state to his total net income from all sources ;

(1) Every fiduciary , except a receiver appointed by au-

individual, shall make under oath a return for any of the fol-

department :
(a) Every individual having a gross income for the taxable
year of seven hundred dollars or over ;
(b) Every estate the gross income of which for the taxable

Section 5. Section 141.300 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) Every individual shall , at the time prescribed in sub-
section (3) of this section , make a declaration of his estimated
tax for the taxable year if his gross income from sources other
than wages upon which Kentucky income tax will be withheld
can reasonably be expected to exceed one hundred dollars for
the taxable year and his gross income to be seven hundred
dollars or more .
(2) In the declaration required under subsection ( 1 ) the
individual shall state :
(a) The amount which he estimates as the amount of tax
under KRS 141.020 for the taxable year ;
(b) The amount which he estimates as the credits for
the taxable year under KRS 141.310 and KRS 141.315 ;

(c) The excess of the amount estimated under subpara-
graph (a) over the amount estimated under subparagraph (b) ,
which excess for purposes of this chapter shall be considered
the estimated tax for the taxable year ;
(d) Such other information as may be prescribed in regu-
lations promulgated by the department .
(3) The declaration required under subsection ( 1 ) of this
section shall be filed with the department on or before April
15 of the taxable year , except that if the requirements of sub-
section (1 ) of this section are first met :
(a) After April 1 and before July 2 of the taxable year ,

of the taxable year ; or

taxable year .
(4) An individual may make amendments of a declaration
filed during the taxable year under subsection (3 ) of this section,
under regulations prescribed by the department .

able year the taxpayer files a return for the taxable year for

scribed by the department :

thereof , such return shall , for the purposes of this section, be
considered as the amendment of the declaration permitted by

CHAPTER 3 49

February 15 .
(6) The department shall promulgate regulations gov-
erning reasonable extensions of time for filing declarations and
paying the estimated tax. Except in the case of taxpayers who
are abroad , no such extension shall be for more than six months .
(7) If the taxpayer is unable to make his own declara-
tion , the declaration shall be made by a duly authorized agent
or by the guardian or other person charged with the care of the
person or property of such taxpayer .
(8) For the purposes of KRS 131.190 , a declaration of
estimated tax shall be held and considered a return of income
under this chapter .

Section 3. Section 341.050 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-

Covered employment . ( 1) As used in this chapter , unless
the context clearly requires otherwise and subject to the other
provisions of this section , "covered employment " means service ,

or under any contract of hire , written or oral , express or implied
in which the relationship of the individual performing such

rendered is , as to those services , the legal relationship of em-
ployer and employe .
(2 ) "Covered employment " includes a worker's entire
service performed within or both within and without this state if:
(a ) The service is localized in this state ; or

the service is performed in this state , and :
1. The base of operations , or , if there is no base of opera-

(3) Services not covered under subsections ( 1 ) or ( 2 ) of
this section, and performed entirely without this state , with
respect to no part of which contributions are required and paid

(1) Service not in the course of the employing unit's trade

the current or the preceding calendar year , has or had in covered
employment four or more workers ( whether the same workers
are or were employed in each such day ) .
( 3 ) Any employing unit that succeeds to the organization ,
trade or business of another employing unit which at the time of
such succession is a subject employer ; and any employing unit
which succeeds to the organization , trade , or business of any
previously separate portion of another employing unit , which
portion , if treated as a separate employing unit , would be, at the
time of the succession , a subject employer under subsections ( 1 )
or ( 2 ) of this section .
(4 ) Any employing unit that succeeds to any of the assets
of a subject employer's business by any means whatever , other-
wise than in the ordinary course of trade , is a subject employer if :
(a ) The transferee has continued or resumed the business
of the transferor , either in the same establishment or elsewhere ;
and
(b ) The transferee has employed substantially the same
workers as those the transferor had employed in connection with
the assets acquired ; and
(c) The transferee has acquired a previously separate por-
tion of the transferror's assets , which portion , if treated as a sep-
arate employing unit , would be, at the time of the succession , a
subject employer under subsections ( 1 ) or ( 2 ) of this section .
(5 ) Any other employing unit which has elected to be-
come subject to this chapter , pursuant to subsection ( 3 ) of KRS
341.250 .

64 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(6) Any employing unit which has in covered employment
within this state workers subject to the Federal Unemployment
Tax Act .
(7 ) Any employing unit which , having become a subject
employer under subsections ( 1 ) to ( 6 ) of this section , has not
ceased to be a subject employer under KRS 341.250 .
( 8 ) Notwithstanding the provisions of this section or any
other provision of this chapter , no employing unit shall be initially
determined a subject employer on the basis of covered employ-
ment performed more than six calendar years prior to the year
in which such determination is made , unless the commissioner
can show that the records of such employment experience were
fraudulently concealed or withheld for the purpose of escaping
liability under this chapter .
Section 6. Section 341.270 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Determination of employer's contribution rate . ( 1 ) Each
subject employer's contribution rate shall be two and seven - tenths
percent except as otherwise provided in this section.
(2) No subject employer's contribution rate shall be less
than two and seven -tenths percent unless :
( a ) Benefits were chargeable to his account with respect to
the thirty -six months immediately preceding the computation
date ; and
(b ) His reserve account balance equals or exceeds five
times the largest amount of benefits charged against it within any
one of the three years last preceding the computation date .
(3 ) Except as otherwise provided in this section , for the
calendar year 1959 and each calendar year thereafter , employer
contribution rates shall be determined in accordance with "Table
A❞ set out in subsection ( 4 ) of this section . Prior to each calendar
year the commissioner shall determine , as of the 31st day of
October , the fund solvency factor by dividing the “benefit cost
ratio " into the "state -wide reserve ratio ." If such factor :
(a ) Equals or exceeds six , the rates listed in "Column B"
of "Table A❞ shall be in effect .
(b ) Equals or exceeds three and one -half but is less than
six , the rates listed in "Column C" of "Table A" shall be in effect .

CHAPTER 4 65
( c) Equals or exceeds two and one-half but is less than
three and one -half, the rates listed in " Column D " of "Table A”
shall be in effect .
( d ) Is less than two and one -half , no subject employer's
contribution rate shall be less than two and seven -tenths percent
and the contribution rate for any subject employer whose indi-
vidual reserve ratio as of the computation date is less than zero
( because benefits have been charged in excess of contributions )
shall be four and two -tenths percent .
(4 ) For the calendar year 1959 and each calendar year
thereafter , contribution rates shall be determined upon the basis
of an individual employer's reserve ratio as of the computation
date and the schedule of rates established under subsection ( 3 )
of this section . Except as otherwise provided in this section, the
contribution rate for each subject employer for the calendar year
immediately following the computation date shall be the rate in
that "Column " of "Table A," as set out below , effective with re-
spect to such calendar year , which appears on the same line as
his reserve ratio as shown in "Column A" of the same table .

(5) As used in this section and elsewhere in this chapter ,
unless the context clearly requires otherwise :
(a) "Trust fund balance" means the amount of money in
the Unemployment Insurance Fund , exclusive of monies trans-
ferred to the fund under the provisions of the Federal Employ-
ment Security Administrative Financing Act of 1954 , which have
been appropriated or are subject to appropriation by the legisla-

66 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ture . In determining the amount in the fund as of a given date
all money received by the division as of such date shall be con-
sidered as being in the fund on such date .
(b ) "Taxable wages" means the payroll or payrolls by
which contributions were measured for a specified period .
(c) "State -wide reserve ratio " means the percentage ratio
obtained by dividing the taxable wages for the twelve months
ending on the 30th day of September into the "trust fund balance"
as of the immediately following 31st day of October .
(d) "Benefit cost ratio " means the percentage ratio ob-
tained by dividing the taxable wages for the sixty months ending
on the 30th day of September , which have been reported to the
division on or before the immediately following 31st day of Oc-
tober , into the total amount of benefits paid from the fund during
that same sixty months period .
(e ) An employer's " reserve ratio" means the percentage
ratio of his reserve account balance as of the computation date to
his taxable payrolls for the thirty - six months last preceding such
computation date .
(f) An employer's " reserve account balance" means the
amount by which contributions credited to his reserve account
exceed benefits charged to such account . If benefits charged to
an account exceed contributions credited to such account as of
the computation date , such account shall be considered as having
a debit balance and a reserve ratio of "less than zero ."
(g) "Computation date " means the date , occurring at least
once in each calendar year and within twenty -seven weeks prior
to the effective date of new rates of contribution , as of which
individual employer contribution rates are computed . The com-
mission shall by regulation prescribe the computation date .
Section 7. Section 341.350 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Conditions of qualification for benefits . An unemployed
worker shall , except as provided in KRS 341.360 and 341.370 , be
eligible for benefits with respect to any week of unemployment
only if:
( 1 ) He has made a claim for benefits ;
(2 ) He has registered for work with respect to such week
in accordance with regulations prescribed by the commission ;

CHAPTER 4 67
(3 ) He is physically and mentally able to work ;
( 4 ) He is available for suitable work , and making such
reasonable effort to obtain work as might be expected of a pru-
dent person under like circumstances ;
( 5 ) His base -period wages in that calendar quarter of his
base period in which such wages were highest are equal to at
least two hundred and fifty dollars , and his total base -period
wages are not less than one and three- eighths times the base-
period wages paid to him in such quarter and he was paid base-
period wages in the last six months of his base period equal to
at least eight times his weekly benefit rate .
(6 ) He has served a waiting period of one week of un-
employment within his benefit year . No week of unemployment
shall be counted as a waiting period unless the worker has satis-
fied the conditions of subsections ( 1 ) , ( 2 ) , ( 3 ) , ( 4 ) and ( 5 ) of
this section .
(7) The foregoing eligibility requirements and the con-
ditions of benefit disqualifications imposed by KRS 341.370 shall
be strictly construed .
Section 8. Section 341.370 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Conditions of benefit disqualification . ( 1 ) According to the
circumstances in each case , a worker shall be disqualified from
receiving benefits or serving a waiting period :
(a ) For not less than one nor more than sixteen weeks im-
mediately following the commission of the disqualifying act, if,
subsequent to his most recent work , he has failed without good
cause either to apply for available , suitable work when so directed
by the employment office or the commissioner or to accept suit-
able work when offered him , or to return to his customary self-
employment when so directed by the commissioner ;
(b ) For not less than six nor more than sixteen weeks im-
mediately following the commission of the disqualifying act , if he
has left his most recent suitable work voluntarily without good
cause ; or
( c ) For not less than six nor more than sixteen weeks im-
mediately following the commission of the disqualifying act , if
he has been discharged for misconduct connected with his most

68 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
recent work , but legitimate activity in connection with labor
organizations or failure to join a company union shall not be
construed as misconduct .
(2 ) A worker shall be disqualified from receiving benefits
or serving a waiting period for the duration of any period of un-
employment with respect to which :
( a) He was discharged for dishonesty in connection with
his most recent work.
(b ) He voluntarily quit his most recent work to marry or
for the purpose of attending school , or to become self-employed .
(3) A worker shall be disqualified from receiving benefits
for any week with respect to which he knowingly made a false
statement to establish his right to or the amount of his benefits ;
and , within the succeeding twenty -four months , for such addi-
tional weeks immediately following the date of such determina-
tion , not to exceed a total of fifty -two , as may be determined by
the commissioner .
(4 ) No worker shall be disqualified under paragraph (b)
or ( c ) of subsection ( 1 ) or subsection ( 2 ) of this section unless
the employer , within a reasonable time as prescribed by commis-
sion regulation , notifies the division and the worker in writing of
the alleged voluntary quitting or the discharge for misconduct ;
provided , however , that nothing in this subsection shall restrict
the right of the commissioner to disqualify a worker whose em-
ployer has refused or failed to notify the division of the alleged
voluntary quitting or discharge for misconduct if such alleged
voluntary quitting or discharge for misconduct is known to the
commissioner prior to the time benefits are paid to such worker ;
provided further , that the exercise of such right by the com-
missioner , in the absence of timely notice from the employer , shall
not relieve the employer's reserve account of benefit charges
under the provisions of KRS 341.530 ( 3 ) .
(5 ) As used in this section and in subsection ( 3 ) of KRS
341.530 , “most recent" work shall be construed as that work
which occurred after the first day of the worker's base period
and which last preceded the week of unemployment with respect
to which benefits are claimed ; except that , if the work last pre-
ceding such week of unemployment was intermittent or tem-

CHAPTER 4 69
porary in nature , most recent work may be construed as that work
last preceding such intermittent or temporary work.

Section 12. Section 341.530 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
Employer reserve accounts . ( 1 ) The division shall main-
tain a reserve account for each subject employer , and shall , except
as provided in KRS 341.590 , credit to such account the total
amount of all contributions paid by the employer on his own be-
half. Nothing in this section or elsewhere in this chapter shall be
construed to grant any employer or individual who is or was in
his employ prior claims or rights to the amounts paid by him into
the fund .
(2 ) Except as provided in subsection ( 3 ) of this section the
benefits paid to an eligible worker for a week of unemployment
shall be charged proportionately against the accounts of those
of his subject employers from whom he has base -period wages .
The proportion so used shall be that ratio which is obtained by
dividing the base -period wages of a worker from such employer
by the total base -period wages of such worker , adjusted to the

CHAPTER 4 73
nearest multiple of one percent . For the purpose of this subsec-
tion a fractional part of a cent shall be disregarded unless it
amounts to one -half cent or more , in which case it shall be in-
creased to one cent.
( 3 ) Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection ( 2 ) of
this section , benefits paid to an eligible worker and chargeable to
an employer's reserve account under such subsection shall be
charged against the pooled account if such worker was dis-
charged by such employer for misconduct connected with his
most recent work for such employer , or voluntarily left his most
recent work with such employer without good cause attributable
to the employment , and the employer within a reasonable time ,
as prescribed by commission regulation , notifies the division , in
writing , of the alleged voluntary quitting or discharge for mis-
conduct .
(4 ) Each subject employer's reserve account shall , unless
terminated , be charged with all benefits paid to eligible workers
which are chargeable to such reserve account under subsection
(2 ) of this section . A subject employer's reserve account shall be
deemed to be terminated if he has ceased to be subject to this
chapter , and his account has been closed and any balance remain-
ing therein has been transferred to the pooled account or to a
successor's account as provided in KRS 341.540 .
(5 ) Any subject employer may at any time make voluntary
payments to the fund , additional to the contributions required
under KRS 341.260 and 341.270 , which shall be credited to his
reserve account , according to the general rules established by the
commission .
Section 13. Section 341.550 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
Pooled account . ( 1 ) The division shall maintain a pooled
account to which shall be credited :
( a ) All worker contributions ;
(b) The emergency amounts diverted from the subject em-
ployers ' contributions as provided in KRS 341.560 ;
(c) All realized earnings and gains on investments of the
fund ;
(d ) Except as provided in KRS 341.540 , any balance re-

maining in the reserve account of any previously subject em-
ployer after such employer has ceased to be subject to this
chapter ;
(e ) Any payments into or amounts in the fund not allocable
to any employer's reserve account ; and
(f) Any payments collected under subsection ( 2 ) of this
section .
( 2 ) Any benefits paid through error shall be charged
against the pooled account . The repayment of benefits paid er-
roneously as provided in subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 341.415 shall be
credited to the pooled account . The pooled account shall be cred-
ited with any sums deducted from future benefits as provided in
KRS 341.415 .
Section 14. Section 341.560 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is repealed .
Section 15. A new section of Chapter 341 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
A lien of the same nature and upon the same property and
having the same force , effect and priorities as that created by
KRS 376.010 and 376.140 in favor of persons performing labor is
hereby created in favor of the division to secure the payment of
contributions and penalties accruing by reason of the labor per-
formed by such persons . Notice of such lien shall be filed and
such lien may be enforced in the same manner and within the
same time after such contributions became due as is provided for
filing and enforcing the liens created in KRS 376.010 and 376.140 .
Section 16. A new section of Chapter 341 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any employing unit refusing to make reports required
under this chapter within ten days after written notice sent by
the division to such employing unit's last known address by cer-
tified mail , notifying such employing unit that this section will be
invoked unless such reports are filed , may be restrained or en-
joined from continuing in business in this state or employing per-
sons herein until such reports have been made .
( 2 ) Any employing unit actively engaged in business in
this state , against which the division holds a judgment for contri-

CHAPTER 4 75
butions due under this chapter and upon which an execution has
been returned unsatisfied , may, after ten days written notice by
the division sent to such employing unit's last known address by
certified mail notifying such employing unit that this section will
be invoked unless such judgment is satisfied , be restrained or en-
joined from continuing in business in this state or employing per-
sons herein until such judgment has been satisfied or until such
employing unit shall have furnished bond with surety approved
by the court in a sum equal to the amount of such judgment , plus
any other unpaid contributions due under this chapter and such
further sum as the court shall deem adequate to protect the di-
vision in the collection of contributions which may become due
for the next ensuing six months , said bond to be conditioned
upon payment of such judgment and contributions upon such
terms and at such time as the court may fix .
(3 ) Proceedings under the provisions of this section may
be instituted upon complaint filed by the division in the Franklin
Circuit Court or any other court of competent jurisdiction in ac-
cordance with Kentucky Rules of Civil Procedure , except that no
bond shall be required of the Commonwealth or the division .

CHAPTER 6
(H. B. 205 )
AN ACT relating to holidays .
WHEREAS , the many Grandmother Clubs of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky , affiliated with the National Federation of Grandmother Clubs
of America , have , in conjunction with the parent organization , the
most laudable purposes and objectives as reasons for their chartering
and continued existence , among them being encouragement of good
citizenship , furtherance of social and educational programs and
dedication to scientific research directed toward the elimination of
children's diseases ;
WHEREAS , Grandmothers , as individuals , hold a very special and in-
violate place in the hierarchy of families and are the object of uni-
versal love , respect and admiration ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. The Governor is authorized and requested to
issue annually a proclamation calling upon the state officials to
display the United States flag on all state and school buildings
and the people of the state to display the flag at their homes ,
lodges , churches , places of business and other suitable places
on the second Sunday in October , known as Grandmother's Day
as a public expression of the love , esteem and reverence in which
grandmothers are held by the citizens of the Commonwealth .
Approved March 4, 1958
CHAPTER 7
( S. B. 107 )
AN ACT relating to the Teachers ' Retirement System , amending section
161.520 and 161.660 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes and creating
additional sections .
WHEREAS , the basic provisions of the Teachers ' Retirement Act were
amended effective July 1, 1955 , to provide larger contributions under
section 161.540 and larger retirement and disability allowances under
sections 161.620 and 161.660 for members of the retirement system
then in active service ; and

78 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , no adequate provision was made for teachers already retired
at July 1, 1955 , nor for the transition from the lower benefits in effect
prior to such date to the more adequate allowances accruing on and
after such date under the aforesaid amendments ; and
WHEREAS , the cost of living continues to increase and financial relief
should be granted in the form of increases in benefits to teachers
already retired , as well as to those who will retire during the afore-
said transition period ;
NOW , THEREFORE , it is declared to be the policy and intention of the
General Assembly to provide an increase in payments to teachers
now retired , and to teachers retiring during the next several years ,
in order to make a transition from the lower allowances previously
provided , to the higher level of benefits that eventually will be at-
tained under the amendments effective July 1, 1955 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) Beginning July 1 next following the enactment of this
Act , each teacher retired prior to July 1 , 1955 , and each teacher
retired thereafter who has not received service credit subse-
quent to that date , shall be entitled to an increase in his retire-
ment or disability allowance equal to thirty per cent of such al-
lowance , and each teacher retired subsequent to said date having
service credit subsequent thereto , shall be entitled to an in-
crease in his retirement or disability allowance on a scale to be
fixed by the board of trustees with the advice of the actuary .
In fixing said scale the board of trustees shall reduce the in-
crease established by this section by an amount not exceeding
ten per cent of the maximum increase for each service credit
year earned subsequent to July 1 , 1955. Such reduction of the
arbitrary increase fixed by this section may be established at
less than the aforesaid ten per cent for each service year's credit
if the board determines that the value to the annuitant of the
increase in the allowance of each service year subsequent to the
aforesaid date is less than the amount of said reduction .
(2 ) In the event the total annual allowance provided
under ( 1 ) of this section is an amount less than the service
credit years of the teacher multiplied by twenty - five dollars ,
then the provision of ( 1 ) shall not apply , but instead the annual
allowance shall be twenty -five dollars per service credit year .

CHAPTER 7 79
(3 ) In the case of teachers retired or retiring under an
option as provided in section 161.630 , Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes, the increases provided under ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of this section
shall be applied in the same proportion to the benefits of the
teacher and his beneficiary or beneficiaries .
(4 ) Increases in retirement allowances provided under
( 1 ) , ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) of this section shall not apply to allowance
accrued by reason of voluntary contributions .
(5 ) No retroactive payments shall be made under ( 1 ) ,
( 2 ) and ( 3 ) of this section prior to the beginning date of such
increases .
( 6) The increases provided in this section shall be pay-
able for the life of the annuitant and in the same manner and
time as provided in 161.640 , Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Section 2. 161.520 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Membership in the retirement system shall be termi-
nated : (a) by retirement for disability or superannuation ; (b) by
death ; (c) by withdrawal ; (d) when a teacher who is absent from
service withdraws his deposits in the fund ; or (e ) when a teacher
is absent from service three out of six consecutive years , unless
the board of trustees in accordance with its rules and regula-
tions grants a longer period , not in excess of six years , of absence
from active service as a teacher without the loss of his status as
a member .
(2) Prior service rights shall be forfeited upon withdrawal ,
and if the teacher again enters the service , it shall be as a new
teacher , except that any teacher who withdraws by claiming his
deposits may , before he is absent from service three school
years , repay to the system the sums he received from it upon
his withdrawal with regular interest and thus re - establish his
prior service rights .
(3) In case of withdrawal from service prior to eligibility
for retirement , the board of trustees shall return to the teacher
all of his accumulated contributions , but the teacher shall have
no claim upon any contributions made by the state with a view
to his retirement . If the member is eligible for a retirement al-
lowance provided by 161.600 , 161.610 or 161.620 , no withdrawal

80 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and refund shall be permitted , but in lieu thereof the teacher
shall be entitled to the retirement or disability allowance pro-
vided by the retirement system .
(4) Except as provided in subsection (5 ) hereunder , in case
of death prior to retirement the board of trustees shall pay to
the estate of the deceased member or to his assigns all of his
accumulated contributions , with regular interest, but the estate
or assigns shall have no claim on any contributions made by the
state with a view to the retirement of the teacher.
(5) ( a) Upon the death of an active member who has
had subsequent service credit for at least six years and who was
in service for at least one full fiscal year immediately prior to
his death or prior to the beginning of his last illness , or upon
the death of a teacher retired for disability , the board of trustees
shall pay to the estate or assigns of the deceased teacher a re-
fund of his accumulated contributions , provided that , if the de-
ceased teacher is survived by a spouse whose marriage to the
deceased teacher was at least one year prior to the teacher's
death , an unmarried child or children under eighteen years of
age , or a dependent parent or parents , such survivor or survivors
in the order named may elect to receive in lieu of the accumulated
contributions a survivors benefit described hereinafter in this
section , payable under the conditions specified .
(b) If the survivor is a spouse , the benefit shall be forty
dollars per month , or one-half the retirement allowance that
the teacher would have received had he been eligible and re-
tired at the date of his death or was receiving as a disabled
teacher at the date of death , whichever is larger . If both a
spouse and unmarried children under eighteen years of age sur-
vive the deceased , the benefits shall be increased by forty dollars
per month in the case of one such child , seventy dollars per
month in the case of two such children , and ninety dollars per
month in the case of three such children , and one hundred
dollars per month in the case of four or more such children .
(c) If no spouse survives the deceased member and a child
or children survive , the benefits payable to the child or children
shall be at the rates specified in (b) above . These benefits shall
apply to legally adopted survivors provided the proceedings for
adoption were initiated at least one year prior to the death of the
member .

CHAPTER 7 81
(d) If no spouse or children survive the deceased member ,
a dependent parent or parents aged sixty-five or over shall be
entitled to receive a benefit of forty dollars per month for each
parent . Dependency of a parent shall be established as of the
date of the death of the member and shall be presumed to exist
if the parent was receiving at least one- half of his support from
the deceased teacher .
(e) The benefit to a child, spouse or dependent parent shall
terminate upon the attainment of age eighteen or the marriage
of the child, or the re-marriage of the spouse or parent , if such
parent was unmarried at the date of the death of the deceased
member . The benefit to a spouse or parent shall not be payable
to any such beneficiary during the time he is receiving an an-
nuity , pension or allowance in excess of forty dollars per month
from a retirement or pension system in the State of Kentucky
financed in whole or in part by public contributions , excluding
Old Age and Survivors Insurance , or is earning more than
eighteen hundred dollars per year from gainful employment .
( f ) The board of trustees shall be the sole judge of eli-
gibility or dependency of any beneficiary , and may require
formal application or information relating thereto , and may sub-
poena records and individuals whenever it deems such action
necessary .
(g) No payments of benefits shall be made unless the board
of trustees shall have authorized such payments . The board
shall formulate and adopt necessary rules and regulations for
the administration of the foregoing provisions and in every case
the decision of the board of trustees shall be final as to eligi-
bility , dependency or disability , and amounts of benefit payable .
Section 3. 161.660 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is

for disability shall be calculated in the same manner as the
allowance for a superannuated member , except that the age

CHAPTER 9
(H. B. 16 )
AN ACT relating to summoning of jurors .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 29.175 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) The clerk of each circuit court shall open the sealed
envelope containing the jury list for a term , or month , not
more than twenty days before the date on which the grand
and petit jurors are required to attend and shall make fair
copies of the jury lists and deliver them to the sheriff in suf-
ficient time to enable him to summon the jurors by the time
hereinafter specified . The sheriff shall , at least ten days prior
to the date on which they are required to attend , summon
the persons to attend . In courts having terms , the judge shall
by order specify to the sheriff the number of jurors to be sum-
moned to attend on the first day of the term , and , if less
than the full number drawn for jury service are directed to
attend on the first day of the term , then the court shall specify
the subsequent day in the term on which the remainder shall
be summoned to attend . In courts of continuous session , the
jurors shall be summoned to attend on the first Monday in the
month for which they are drawn or on such day as is ordered
by the judge ; provided , that the judge by order may direct
the sheriff to summon less than the full number drawn to at-
tend the first Monday, and to summon the remainder at a
subsequent day . The officer may summon such persons by
mail . The summons shall be sealed in an envelope addressed
to the prospective juror and mailed to the juror's address
by registered or certified mail with return receipt requested .
When the return receipt has been received by the sheriff , the
service shall be deemed complete .
(2 ) The list shall be returned by the sheriff seven days
in advance of the day upon which the jurors are required to
attend , with a certificate thereon of the date and manner in
which each juror was summoned . From this list the regular
panels of grand and petit juries shall be selected . The court
may , in selecting the respective panels , interrogate such per-

86 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sons relative to any matter which it deems pertinent and ma-
terial to determine their qualifications as jurors .
( 3 ) If the sheriff's return discloses that a persons sum-
moned for jury service is deceased , or was otherwise unable
to be served , the court , when the jury list is returned , may
draw from the drum an equivalent number of names and the
sheriff shall immediately summon such persons .
(4) No sheriff or other officer shall corruptly or through
favor or
illwill
, summon
a juror with intent that the juror
shall find
averdict for

,

any particular individual
, and he shall not
, through favor
or
illwill
, fail
tosummon
aperson
toact
as
ajuror
.

(2 ) Willful concealment of unpurchased merchandise of

reasonable manner for a reasonable length of time .

Approved March 10 , 1958
AN ACT relating to vocational rehabilitation services to the blind .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. (1 ) There is created within the State Rehabili-
tation Agency in the Department of Education a division of
services for the blind .
(2) The division shall consist of a director and such other
employes as the Department of Education shall determine .
Section 2. There is created an advisory committee to advise
the director concerning the activities of the division . The com-
mittee shall consist of not less than five members appointed by
the Governor for terms of four years , or until their successors
are appointed and qualified . At least two members of the com-
mittee shall be blind persons who have a known interest in and
a demonstrated knowledge of the problems and needs of the
blind . No state employe shall be a member of the committee .
Members of the committee shall serve without compensation ,

88 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
but shall be reimbursed for actual expenses incurred in service
on the committee .
Section 3. Section 163.150 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The State Rehabilitation Agency in the Department
of Education , with the approval of the Superintendent of Public
Instruction and the State Board of Education shall establish
and maintain a business enterprise program for the blind . In
connection therewith the agency shall be authorized to own,
lease , manage, supervise and operate vending stands and other
business enterprises for the use or benefit of blind persons in
federal , state , private and other buildings .
(2) The share of expenses of this program to be charged
to each enterprise shall not exceed three percent of the gross
receipts of such enterprise above the amount of one hundred
and twenty - five dollars gross sales per week , the first one
hundred and twenty -five dollars per week being exempt from
any such fee.
(3) One or more stand placement agents shall be em-
ployed to locate and establish additional vending stands .
Section 4. ( 1 ) To meet the needs and special problems
of the blind , the portion of the budget provided for vocational
rehabilitation services for the blind and the apportionment for
other vocational rehabilitation services shall be separately es-
tablished and maintained .
(2) All vocational rehabilitation services for the blind ,
under both state and federal appropriation , shall be channeled
through the division of services for the blind .

CHAPTER 14
(H. B. 15 )
AN ACT changing the classification of the city of Hardinsburg , in Breck-
inridge county .
WHEREAS ,satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the city of Hardinsburg , in Breckin-
ridge county , is such as to justify its being classified as a city of the
fifth class .
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
The city of Hardinsburg , in Breckinridge county , is trans-
ferred from the sixth to the fifth class of cities .
Approved March 14 , 1958
CHAPTER 15
(H. B. 35 )
AN ACT relating to the standard of time and to be known as the Stith-
Hamilton Act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 2.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
(1 ) The standard time fixed and prescribed for the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky by Act of Congress or by order of the
Interstate Commerce Commission shall apply to and govern all
laws , regulations and rules relating to the time of performance
of any act by any officer or department of the Commonwealth ,
or of any county , city or subdivision or agency thereof , or re-
lating to the time that any right shall accrue or determine , or
within which any act shall or shall not be performed by any
person subject to the jurisdiction of the Commonwealth , and in
all the public schools and institutions of the Commonwealth and
on the public works of the Commonwealth or any county , city
or district thereof , or in all contracts or choses in action made
or to be performed in the Commonwealth .

CHAPTER 16 91
(2) No person, firm or corporation operating or maintain-
ing a place of business of any kind shall employ , display , or
maintain any other standard of time than that provided in this
section .
Section 2. Any person who violates KRS 2.160 shall be
fined not less than twenty -five dollars not more than five hundred
dollars , or imprisoned not less than ten days nor more than thirty
days , or both . Each day of such violation shall be a separate
offense .
Section 3. No county , city , town , municipal corporation ,
agency or instrumentality of the Commonwealth shall employ any
time or adopt any ordinance , resolution or order providing for the
use of any time other than that specified in KRS 2.160 .
Section 4. The Commission of Finance shall not draw any
warrant upon the state treasury for the payment of Common-
wealth funds to any county , city , town , municipal corporation ,
agency or instrumentality or the Commonwealth which uses or
employs any time or which has adopted or hereafter adopts any
ordinance , resolution or order providing for the use of any time
other than that specified in KRS 2.160 .
Approved March 14 , 1958

CHAPTER 18
Approved March 14 , 1958
(H. B. 83 )
AN ACT relating to highway construction .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 176.120 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
The Department of Highways may cause any road construc-
tion or reconstruction work to be done by employes of the de-
partment instead of through contractors when , in the opinion of
the commissioner as expressed in an official order :
(a ) No satisfactory bid is received for the construction or
reconstruction of the road after all necessary steps have been
taken to obtain bids for such work ; or
(b ) Emergency construction or reconstruction is neces-
sary; or
(c ) Construction or reconstruction of access roads would
serve as an inducement to industrial location or substantial ex-
pansion ; or
(d ) The work project is within the boundaries of property
owned by other governmental agencies ; or
(e) The estimated cost of the project is less than $ 12,000 .
Approved March 14 , 1958

CHAPTER 20 93
CHAPTER 19
(H. B. 111 )
AN ACT changing the classification of the city of Lynnview in Jeffer-
son county .
WHEREAS , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the city of Lynnview , in Jefferson
county , is such as to justify its being assigned to the fifth class of
cities .
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
That the city of Lynnview , in Jefferson county , is hereby
transferred from the sixth to the fifth class of cities .
Approved March 14 , 1958
CHAPTER 20
(H. R. 11 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION directing payment of a claim against the Com-
monwealth in favor of the Wesley Chapel of the Methodist Church
at Fearisville in Lewis county , Kentucky .
WHEREAS , as the result of the construction of a bridge by the Depart-
ment of Highways across Frye Branch near the Wesley Chapel of the
Methodist Church at Fearisville in Lewis county , Kentucky , the
natural flow of water has been impeded and diverted as to cause the
waters of the branch to flood the church building , doing substantial
damage and requiring repairs in the amount of $ 1200 ; and
WHEREAS , the Department of Highways has recognized the merit of
the claim of the church for damages based upon negligence and im-
proper construction of the bridge and fills , and it appears that the
claim of the church is just and proper and should be paid ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
That the Commissioner of Highways is authorized and
directed to prepare and submit to the Commissioner of Finance
an appropriate authorization for payment of the sum of $ 1200
to the Wesley Chapel of the Methodist Church at Fearisville in
Lewis county , and the Commissioner of Finance upon receipt
of such authorization is directed to issue a warrant for payment
of same , out of the State Road Fund .
Approved March 14 , 1958

CHAPTER 24
Approved March 17 , 1958
(H. B. 140 )
AN ACT relating to construction of National Guard Armories , and appro-
priating money therefor .
WHEREAS , the General Assembly in 1956 enacted the National Guard
Armory Construction Act , Chapter 71 of the 1958 Acts of the General
Assembly , pursuant to which appropriation was made to the Gov-
ernor's Special Emergency Fund of whatever surplus remained in the
General Expenditure Fund at the end of the 1956-1957 fiscal year ,
not to exceed the sum of Five Hundred Seventy - one Thousand ( $ 571 ,-
000,000 ) Dollars , for the construction of National Guard Armories ;
and
WHEREAS , at the close of the 1956-1957 fiscal year there was a sufficient
surplus in the General Expenditure Fund , so that by the operation of
the Act referred to the entire sum of Five Hundred Seventy - one
Thousand ( 571,000 ) Dollars became available to the Governor's
Special Emergency Fund ; and
WHEREAS , pursuant to the provisions of Chapter 71 of the 1956 Acts
of the General Assembly , this appropriation was made available for
the purposes of the Act until expended , or until the close of the
fiscal year 1957-1958 , whichever should occur first ; and

96 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , matching funds to be provided by the federal government
have not been made available in time to award contracts or to com-
plete the work of construction prior to the close of the fiscal year
1957-1958 on June 30 , 1958 ; and
WHEREAS , considerable time and effort have been expended in pro-
curing sites and in planning for the construction of National Guard
Armories ; and
WHEREAS , the urgent need for the construction of these National Guard
Armories still exists ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 36.082 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) This section may be cited as the National Guard Ar-
mory Construction Act .
(2 ) In enacting this section , it is the intention of the Gen-
eral Assembly to provide funds for the construction of National
Guard Armories at the following locations : Olive Hill , Jackson ,
Barbourville , Louisville , Shively , Bardstown , Carrollton , Bran-
denburg , Hopkinsville , Tompkinsville , Marion , Monticello , Mid-
dlesboro , and Glasgow .
(3) As used in this section , unless the context requires
otherwise :
(a) "Armory" means a structure in which to house , admin-
ister , train , and equip a unit or units of the Kentucky National
Guard and all appurtenances necessary thereto .
(b) "Federal funds" means funds provided by the Congress
of the United States through the enactment of public law for
the construction of National Guard Armories in the several
states .
(4 ) To carry out the purposes of this section , the funds
in the amount of Five Hundred Seventy -one Thousand
( $ 571,000.00 ) Dollars appropriated to the Governor's Special
Emergency Fund by Chapter 71 of the 1956 Acts of the General
Assembly for the construction of National Guard Armories , or any
amount thereof remaining unencumbered and unexpended on
June 30 , 1958 , shall be carried forward and is hereby appropriated
to the Capital Construction Fund and made available for ex-

CHAPTER 25 97
penditure during the fiscal years beginning on July 1 , 1958 , and
expiring on June 30 , 1960 .
CHAPTER 25
Approved March 18 , 1958
(H. B. 270 )
AN ACT relating to compensation of circuit judges , making an appro-
priation therefor .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. The compensation of circuit judges , payable
out of the state treasury , shall be eight thousand four hundred
dollars per annum for each judge .
Section 2. Section 64.500 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The fiscal court of any one or more counties located in
any judicial district may allow and pay to the judge or judges
of the circuit court of the district such annual sum as will reim-
burse the judge or judges for such incidental and office expenses
incurred in discharging the duties of the office , including , but
not limited to , office rental , lights and heat , automobile expenses
not reimbursable under KRS 64.505 , stenographic services , post-
age , telephone service , supplies , stationery , books and periodicals ,
as the fiscal court deems just and proper . Two or more counties
in the same district may prorate or divide any allowance for
such expenses .
Section 3. Nothing contained in this Act shall be con-
strued to repeal or modify the provisions of KRS 64.505 or the
provisions of KRS 24.270 .
Section 4. In addition to other appropriations made, to
the judiciary , there is hereby appropriated out of the General
Fund to the judiciary for salaries and expenses of circuit judges
the sum of $ 52,200 for each of the fiscal years 1958-1959 , and
1959-1960 , and to the judiciary for the circuit judges ' retire-
ment system the sum of $ 1,044 for each of the fiscal years
1958-1959 and 1959-1960 .
Approved March 18 , 1958

( S. B. 49 )
AN ACT relating to physical therapy and the practice thereof , including
establishment of a Physical Therapy Board , collection of fees , and
the issuance , suspension and revocation of licenses , and prescribing
penalties for unlicensed practice of physical therapy and other viola-
tions of this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. Definitions . As used in this Act, unless the con-
text otherwise requires :
(a ) "Physical Therapy " means the treatment of any bodily
or mental condition of any person by the use of physical , chemical
or mechanical agents ; the properties of heat , light , water , elec-
tricity ; massage ; and therapeutic exercises ,
allunder the prescrip-

any person licensed in this state according to law, from
carrying out the therapy or practice for which he is duly
licensed .
Provided further that persons regularly employed by the
United States or in mental hospitals operated by the Common-
wealth of Kentucky shall be exempted from the provisions of
this Act while engaged in such employment .
(b) A licensed physical therapist may hold himself out as
a "Physical Therapist " or "Licensed Physical Therapist " and may
use the abbreviations "P. T. " or "L. P. T. " as a part of or imme-
diately following his name , in connection with his profession . No
person who is not the holder of a license from the State Board
of Physical Therapy shall so use the terms " Physical Therapist " or
"Licensed Physical Therapist " or the abbreviation " L. P. T." and
no person other than licensees of said board or physio -therapists
licensed by the State Board of Health shall so use the abbrevia-
tion "P. T."
Section 3. Board of Physical Therapy Created . There is
hereby established a State Board of Physical Therapy which shall
consist of three members who shall be appointed by the Governor
from a list of five persons submitted by the Kentucky Chapter of
the American Physical Therapy Association , all of whom shall be
residents of Kentucky and shall have engaged in the practice of
physical therapy within this state for at least two years . Members
of the first board shall be appointed for terms of one , two , and
three years , respectively . Subsequent appointments shall be for
a term of two years and until their successors are appointed and
qualify . Vacancies shall be filled by the Governor in like manner
from a list of five submitted by the Kentucky Chapter of the
American Physical Therapy Association . The board shall an-
nually elect a chairman and a secretary .
Section 4. Duties of Board . It shall be the duty of the State
Board of Physical Therapy to receive applications from persons
desiring to become physical therapists and to determine whether
said applicants meet the qualifications and standards required by
this Act of all physical therapists . The board shall also be
charged with enforcement of the provisions of this Act .
The board is hereby constituted a body corporate , with

to institute civil proceedings to enjoin violation thereof , or both .

accompanied by an application fee of $ 25.00 , no part of which
shall be returned . If it appears from said application that the
applicant possesses the qualifications required by this Act, such
applicant shall be admitted to the next scheduled written ex-
amination in which his knowledge of anatomy, neuro -anatomy ,
kinesiology , physiology , pathology , psychology , neurology , med-
ical ethics, technical procedures in the practice of physical
therapy , and such other subjects as the board may determine to
be necessary , shall be tested.
Examinations shall be held twice a year ; in October and
April , at Frankfort . Applicants who are admitted to the written
examination may be granted temporary licenses which shall be
valid until said applicants ' examinations are graded and they are
notified by the board of their score thereon . All applicants who
receive an average score of not less than 75 percentage points on
the entire examination and not less than 65 percentage points on
each subject in which examined shall be licensed as physical
therapists and furnished with a certificate attesting thereto . This
license shall be automatically renewed annually , without further
examination , upon payment , on or before January 31 of each year ,
of a renewal fee to be fixed by the board in an amount not to
exceed $ 10.00 . Licenses which are not renewed by that date shall
lapse , except that the board may reinstate a lapsed license upon
payment of the renewal fee. Applicants who fail to receive a
passing score on their examination shall not be licensed ; but the
board may, by regulation , permit applicants to take the exami-
nation more than once .
Section 6. Registration Without Examination . The State
Board of Physical Therapy may register as a physical therapist
and may furnish a license without examination to any person who
applies for registration on or before December 31 , 1958 , and who
at the time of the passage of this Act , is practicing physical ther-
apy in this state , and who possesses the qualifications required
by this Act of

asphysical therapists
in
this state

ofpersons licensed

or foreign country are determined by the board to be as high as
those of this state .
All applications for licensure without examination shall be
accompanied by an application fee of $ 25.00 , no part of which
shall be returned .
Section 7. Refusal , Revocation and Suspension of a License .
The board , after due notice and hearing may refuse to license
any applicant , and may refuse to renew the license of any licensed
person, and may suspend or revoke the license of any licensed
person
( 1 ) Who indulges in excessive use of alcoholic beverages or
who is addicted to the use of narcotic drugs ;
(2 ) Who has been convicted of violating any state or
federal narcotic law;
(3) Who is , in the judgment of the board , guilty of im-
moral or unprofessional conduct ;
(4) Who has been convicted of a felony or any crime in-
volving moral turpitude ;
(5) Who has obtained or attempted to obtain a license by
fraud or material misrepresentation or who has made any other
false statement to the board ;
( 6 ) Who is guilty , in the judgment of the board , of gross
negligence in his practice of physical therapy ;
(7) Who has been declared insane by a court of competent
jurisdiction and has not thereafter been declared lawfully sane ;
( 8 ) Who has treated or undertaken to treat human ail-
ments otherwise than by physical therapy as defined in this Act ,
or who has undertaken to practice independent of the prescrip-
tion , direction or supervision of a licensed medical doctor .
( 9 ) Who has failed or refused to obey any lawful order or
regulation of the board .
Section 8. Expense of Board . All fees collected by the board
shall be paid into the state treasury and credited to an account
for the payment of all lawful expenses of the board . Each mem-
ber of the board shall be entitled to a per diem of $ 10.00 and his
reasonable expenses for attending each meeting of the board , and
the board may employ such clerical assistance and purchase such
materials and supplies as it may deem necessary for the proper

pipes , structural members or fixtures and other essential por-
tions of municipal buildings , ( e ) the initial equipment of any
newly -acquired facility wherein any essential governmental
function of the municipality may be located or carried on , (f)
the purchase and installation of traffic control devices and
fire alarm equipment , ( g ) the reconstruction and resurfacing ,
but not routine maintenance , of streets and other public ways ,
(h) the acquisition of motorized equipment purchased as addi-
tions to , but not replacements for , existing equipment , and ,
(i ) engineering and other costs incurred by the city in connec-
tion with the construction of public improvements financed
under a special assessment plan or plans authorized in Chapter
93 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
( 5 ) Ad valorem taxes for the benefit of the sinking fund
shall not be levied unless the income of the sinking fund is
otherwise insufficient to meet such requirements .
(6 ) Licenses shall be issued and enforced on such terms
and conditions as may be prescribed by ordinance .
(7) If any section , paragraph , clause or provision of this
statute shall be held invalid , the invalidity of such section ,
paragraph , clause , or provision shall not affect any of the re-
maining provisions .
Approved March 24 , 1958
