CHAPTER 78
( S. B. 181 )
AN ACT relating to the elimination of hazards at railroad - highway cross-
ings by means of grade separation structures or automatic warning
devices and providing for allocation and apportionment of the costs
of construction , installation and maintenance thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. The entire construction costs of projects for the
elimination of hazards of railroad -highway crossings , including
the separation or protection of grades at crossings , the reconstruc-
tion of existing railroad grade crossing structures , and the re-
location of highways to eliminate railroad grade crossings which
may be paid for in whole or part from state funds , including , but
not limited to , the cost of preparing the plans and specifications
and supervising the improvement , the acquisition of necessary
property , the construction of approaches, drainage structures ,
roadways and pavements , accommodations for public utilities ,
and damages paid to abutting property owners , shall be allocated
between the railroad involved , and the governmental unit or
units involved in the same ratio as the net benefit received by
such railroad from the project bears to the net benefit accruing
to the public using the highway , and in no case shall the net bene-
fit to any railroad or railroads be deemed to be more than 10%
of the total benefit resulting from the project . The Department
of Highways shall be responsible for determining the proportion
of the benefits derived by the railroad from the project , and shall
fix standards for the determining of said benefits which shall be
consistent with the standards adopted for similar purposes by the
United States Bureau of Public Roads under the Federal -Aid-
Highway Act of 1944. The cost of maintenance of all structures
for the elimination of railroad grade crossings , and of installations
for the protection of existing grade crossings , constructed or in-
stalled under this Act, shall be borne by the governmental unit

418 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or units constructing them . Before any state funds shall be ex-
pended for any project constructed under this Act , a contract
shall be entered into between the railroad and the governmental
unit or units involved setting forth the duties of each as to con-
struction and maintenance of the project .
Section 2. All statutes in conflict herewith are hereby re-
pealed .
CHAPTER 79
Approved March 28 , 1958
( S. B. 209)
AN ACT changing the classification of the city of West Point , in Hardin
county .
WHEREAS , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the city of West Point , in Hardin
county , is such as to justify its being classified as a city of the fifth
class .
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
The city of West Point , in Hardin county , is transferred from
the sixth to the fifth class of cities .
Approved March 28 , 1958
CHAPTER 80
( S. B. 222)
AN ACT relating to the gift or bequest of dead bodies or parts thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. Any person in the Commonwealth of the age of
twenty -one years or more and of sound mind may, by will or
by a written instrument executed in the presence of at least two
attesting witnesses , give , grant or bequest his body to any pro-
fessor or medical school or college authorized by KRS 311.300
to 311.350 to procure or receive dead bodies for educational or
scientific purposes , or may give , grant or bequeath any part of

CHAPTER 81 419
his body to any responsible person or group of persons, natural
or corporate , whom the State Board of Health finds is equipped
to properly receive and preserve the same for educational or
scientific study or to transplant into the body or bodies of living
persons . Any such disposition of his body or part thereof may
be revoked by the testator or grantor at any time prior to his
death by the execution of a written instrument in the same man-
ner as the original legacy or grant .
Section 2. Any person or persons having the right to a
body for burial may likewise consent to such use of the body
or part thereof as set forth in section 1 .
Section 3. For the purpose of this Act, the persons having
the right to dispose of a body are as follows :
( 1 ) The surviving spouse , if any , and if none ,
(2 ) The person or persons who are entitled to the real
property of the deceased under KRS 391.010 , and if none ,
( 3 ) The person , agency , institution , or organization hav-
ing the obligation to bury such body .
Section 4. The State Board of Health shall , by regulation ,
fix reasonable standards with respect to qualifications , equip-
ment , facilities and experience to be required of persons desiring
to qualify to receive parts of human bodies , as hereinabove
provided . The board may , if it deems it desirable , include in
such regulation a requirement that authorized recipients of parts
of human bodies post a bond conditioned upon compliance with
all the terms and conditions of said regulation .
CHAPTER 81
( S. B. 229 )
Approved March 28 , 1958
AN ACT relating to the compensation of justices of the peace and declar-
ing an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. In enacting legislation relating to justices of the
peace , it is the intention of the General Assembly to enable jus-
tices of the peace in counties having a population of less than
250,000 to exercise criminal jurisdiction as set out in KRS 25.010 ,

420 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and to provide for compensation for their performance of the
new duties thereby imposed upon them .
Section 2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) In counties having a population of less than 250,000
each justice of the peace shall be exclusively compensated for the
performance of the duties of his office , in so far as they relate to
the trial or decision of criminal cases , by a salary not to exceed
$ 1,200.00 per annum for counties having a population of 30,000
or less ; $ 2,400.00 per annum for counties having a population
in excess of 30,000 and not exceeding 60,000 ; $ 3,600.00 per
annum for counties having a population in excess of 60,000 and
not exceeding 250,000 . The fiscal court of each county is
authorized to fix salaries pursuant to the above restrictions . Not-
withstanding provisions of KRS 64.530 , salaries for incumbent
justices of the peace shall be set as soon as may be practical
after passage of the Act . Salaries shall be paid , in equal monthly
installments , out of the county treasury .
(2 ) All moneys received or collected in criminal cases on
account of or resulting from the performance of the duties or
the exercise of the powers incident to the office of justice of the
peace , shall be reported and paid in full to the circuit clerk of
the county , on or before the tenth of each succeeding month , for
proper distribution to the county and state .
Section 3. WHEREAS , justices of the peace in counties not
containing a population of more than 250,000 do not now exer-
cise any criminal jurisdiction , an emergency is declared to exist ,
and this Act shall become effective upon
itspassage and approval
by the Governor
.
Approved March 28
, 1958

Section 2. Section 186.030 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The application form shall contain spaces for the
names and post office addresses of the owner and of the lien.
holder as shown by chattel mortgage , or other instrument con-

erty, the application form shall contain space for the declared

weight of the vehicle and the heaviest load that the vehicle will
be used to carry at any time on the highways .
(2 ) The application , when completed , shall be presented
to the county clerk and accompanied by the appropriate fee .
Section 3. Section 186.040 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Upon receiving the application and fee , the county
clerk shall issue to the owner a registration receipt containing the
information required by subsection ( 2 ) and a registration plate .
If the department finds that there is a shortage of materials suit-
able for making plates , or that a substantial saving will result ,
it may require by regulation with the approval of the Governor
that previously issued plates continue to be used for a designated
period . For services performed , the owner shall pay the county
clerk the sum of fifty cents for each registration in addition to
the registration fee .
( 2 ) The registration receipt shall contain the registration
number , the names and post office addresses of the owner and
of the lien holder as shown by chattel mortgage, or other instru-
ment construed as such in this state , on file in the office of the
county clerk and such other information as the department may
require . The county clerk shall be paid a fee of sixty cents for
recording each lien on the registration receipt in addition to
statutory recording fees for recording the chattel mortgage , or
other instrument construed as such in this state .

CHAPTER 82 423
(3 ) If a motor vehicle , other than a new motor vehicle ,
is registered in this state under the authority of a bill of sale ,

the title or the registration number and the name of the state

in addition to the other required information .
Section 4. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) Whenever the owner of a motor vehicle , properly
registered in this state , executes a lien thereon , he shall deliver
the registration receipt to the lienor . The lienor shall , within ten
days thereafter , present such receipt , together with the lien
instrument and proper fees , to the county clerk of the county in
which the motor vehicle is registered for recording in accordance
with KRS 382.670 and KRS 186.040 .
(2 ) The county clerk shall , within five days , mail the
registration receipt with the lien recorded thereon to the owner ,
and shall also record the lien on the copy of the registration
receipt on file in the county clerk's office .
( 3 ) ( a ) When a chattel mortgage , or other instrument
construed as such in this state , is assigned or discharged pur-
suant to the law, the owner shall present the registration receipt
to the lienholder who shall record thereon in language prescribed
by the department that the lien has been assigned or discharged .
The recording shall show the date thereof and shall be signed
by the lienholder or his authorized agent and acknowledged be-
fore an officer authorized to administer an oath .
(b ) When a discharge or assignment has been recorded on
the owner's registration receipt as provided in ( 3 ) ( a ) , the
owner may present the registration receipt to the county clerk
who shall record the information on the copy of the receipt
which contains the lien information on file in the county clerk's
office , for which services the owner shall pay the clerk a fee of
twenty -five cents .
(4 ) For failure to present the registration receipt and
record the lien instrument within the time prescribed , the lienor
shall pay a penalty of two dollars to the county clerk as a pre-
requisite for recording the lien information on the registration

424 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
receipt . The penalty , less the county clerk's commission of five
percent , shall be remitted to the department .
( 5 ) No lien executed prior to January 1 , 1960 , shall be
recorded on the registration receipt issued by the county clerk .
Section 5. Section 186.170 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :

Section 10. A newA new section of the Kentucky Revised

No motor vehicle , not next previously registered in this

Section 11. A new section of Chapter 382 of the Kentucky

Section 12. There is hereby appropriated to the Depart-

able July 1 , 1959 , to facilitate preparation of forms and procedures
necessary for the administration of this Act .

CHAPTER 83 427
CHAPTER 83
( S. B. 255 )
AN ACT relating to gifts of securities and money to minors and making
uniform the law with reference thereto .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act , unless the context other-
wise requires :
( 1 ) "Adult " is a person who has attained the age of twenty-
one years;
(2 ) " Bank " includes every corporation authorized by law
to do a banking business ; ( KRS 287.010 ( a ) ) ;
(3) " Broker " is a person lawfully engaged in the business
of effecting transactions in securities for the account of others .
The term includes a bank which effects such transactions . The
term also includes a person lawfully engaged in buying and sell-
ing securities for his own account , through a broker or otherwise ,
as a part of a regular business ;
(4 ) "Court " means the chancery division of the circuit
court ;
( 5 ) "The custodial property " includes :
( a ) all securities and money under the supervision of
the same custodian for the same minor as a consequence of a
gift or gifts made to the minor in a manner prescribed in this
Act;
(b ) the income from the custodial property ; and

Section 3. ( 1 ) A gift made in a manner prescribed in this

(2 ) By making a gift in a manner prescribed in this Act ,
the donor incorporates in his gift all the provisions of this Act
and grants to the custodian , and to any issuer, transfer agent ,
bank , broker or third person dealing with a person designated as
custodian , the respective powers , rights and immunities provided
in this Act.
Section 4. ( 1 ) The custodian shall collect , hold , manage ,
invest and reinvest the custodial property .
(2 ) The custodian shall pay over to the minor for expendi-
ture by him , or expend for the minor's benefit , so much of or all
the custodial property as the custodian deems advisable for the
support , maintenance , education and benefit of the minor in the
manner, at the time or times , and to the extent that the custodian
in his discretion deems suitable and proper , with or without court
order , with or without regard to the duty of himself or of any
other person to support the minor or his ability to do so , and with
or without regard to any other income or property of the minor
which may be applicable or available for any such purpose .
( 3 ) The court , on the petition of a parent or guardian of
the minor or of the minor , if he has attained the age of fourteen
years , may order the custodian to pay over to the minor for
expenditure by him or to expend so much of or all the custodial
property as is necessary for the minor's support , maintenance or
education .
(4 ) To the extent that the custodial property is not so
expended, the custodian shall deliver or pay it over to the minor
on his attaining the age of twenty - one years or , if the minor dies
before attaining the age of twenty - one years , he shall thereupon
deliver or pay it over to the estate of the minor .
(5 ) The custodian , notwithstanding statutes restricting in-
vestments by fiduciaries , shall invest and reinvest the custodial
property as would a prudent man of discretion and intelligence
who is seeking a reasonable income and the preservation of his
capital , except that he may, in his discretion and without liability
to the minor or his estate , retain a security given to the minor in
a manner prescribed in this Act .

(4 ) Except as otherwise provided in this Act , a custodian

(5 ) A custodian not compensated for his services is not

custodial property provided in this Act .
Section 6. No issuer , transfer agent , bank , broker or other
person acting on the instructions of or otherwise dealing with

money or other property paid or delivered to him.
Section 7. ( 1 ) Only an adult member of the minor's fam-
ily , a guardian of the minor or a trust company is eligible to
become successor custodian . A successor custodian has all the
rights , powers , duties and immunities of a custodian designated
in a manner prescribed by this Act .
(2 ) A custodian , other than the donor , may resign and
designate his successor by :
(a) executing an instrument of resignation designating the
successor custodian ; and
( b ) causing each security which is custodial property and
in registered form to be registered in the name of the successor
custodian followed , in substance , by the words : “as custodian
for ( name of minor ) ---- under the Uniform Gifts to Minors
Act"; and

CHAPTER 83 433
( c) delivering to the successor custodian the instrument
of resignation , each security registered in the name of the suc-
cessor custodian and all other custodial property , together with
any additional instruments required for the transfer thereof .
(3 ) A custodian , whether or not a donor , may petition the
court for permission to resign and for the designation of a suc-
cessor custodian .
(4 ) If the person designated as custodian is not eligible ,
renounces or dies before the minor attains the age of twenty -one
years , the guardian of the minor shall be successor custodian . If
the minor has no guardian , a donor , his legal representative , the
legal representative of the custodian , an adult member of the
minor's family , or the minor , if he has attained the age of fourteen
years , may petition the court for the designation of a successor
custodian .
(5 ) A donor , the legal representative of a donor , an adult
member of the minor's family , a guardian of the minor or the
minor , if he has attained the age of fourteen years , may petition
the court that , for cause shown in the petition , the custodian be
removed and a successor custodian be designated or , in the alter-
native , that the custodian be required to give bond for the per-
formance of his duties .
(6 ) Upon filing of a petition as provided in this sec-
tion , the court shall grant an order to show cause why the relief
prayed for in the petition should not be granted and , in due
course , grant such relief as the court finds to be in the best
interests of the minor .
Section 8. ( 1 ) The minor , if he has attained the age of
fourteen years , or the legal representative of the minor , an adult
member of the minor's family , or a donor or his legal representa-
tive may petition the court for an accounting by the custodian
or his legal representative .
(2 ) The court , in a proceeding under this Act or other-
wise , may require or permit the custodian or his legal representa-
tive to account and , if the custodian is removed , shall so require
and order delivery of all custodial property to the successor cus-
todian and the execution of all instruments required for the
transfer thereof .

AN ACT relating to state purchases giving preference to products of the
Kentucky Industries for the Blind and to be known as the Patience
Vice Act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Where the products of the blind produced under the super-
vision and direction of the Kentucky Industries for the Blind meet
the requirements of state offices , departments , institutions and
agencies of the Commonwealth as to quality , quantity and price ,
such products shall have preference in all purchases by and for
such state offices , departments , institutions and agencies . The
price charged for the products shall , in no case , exceed the price
quoted to other consumers . The Bureau of Rehabilitation Serv-
ices in the Department of Education shall , from time to time ,
cause to be made available to the Division of Purchases , lists of
the products of the Kentucky Industries of the Blind .

CHAPTER 92
(H. B. 550 )
AN ACT relating to municipalities and public utility facilities therein .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. "Municipality" means any county , city , town ,
village and municipal corporation of any and every class in the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , and any board , commission or
agency thereof .
Section 2. No municipality , in which there is located an
existing electric , water or gas public utility plant or facility shall
construct or cause to be constructed any similar utility plant
or any similar public utility facility duplicating such existing
plant or facility or to obtain or acquire any similar public utility
plant or facility other than by the purchase of the existing plant
or facility or by the acquisition of such existing plant or facility
by the exercise of the power of eminent domain .
Section 3. All laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith
to the extent of such conflict are repealed .
Approved March 28 , 1958

AN AST relating to fire protection .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. In enacting a fire department aid law it is
the intention of the General Assembly to supplement local ef-
forts to prevent the loss of lives and property by fire . The
inadequacy of present facilities , the high cost of acquisition
and maintenance of modern fire fighting equipment and the
need for better trained firemen are hereby declared to be
matters of public interest and concern and vital to the pro-
motion of the health , safety , prosperity and security of the
inhabitants of the Commonwealth .

an annualSection 5. The commissioner shall allot on
basis the funds appropriated for fire department aid in
amounts not to exceed $ 500 to those cities of the third to
sixth classes , fire prevention districts organized pursuant to
KRS Chapter 75 and county districts established under au-
thority of KRS 67.320 , in which a fire department is maintained
or being established . With the advice of the Fire Department
Committee , he shall prescribe regulations and standards for
determining the amounts to be granted . Such standards shall
be based on the number of firemen , both paid and volunteer ,
the amount of equipment , housing facilities available and such
other matters or standards as will best effect the purposes of
the fire department aid law . No allotment shall exceed the
total value of the funds , equipment , lands and buildings made
available to the local fire units from any source whatever for
the year in which the allotment is made .
Section 6. Funds alloted by the commissioner shall be
expended by direction of the governing body of the city or dis-

chase , maintenance and repair of fire apparatus and equip-
ment including buildings housing such fire departments . Cities
of the fifth and sixth classes , or fire prevention district , with
population not exceeding 3,000 , may expend allotted funds for
acquisition of lands or buildings , and for expenses of firemen
in attending schools and conventions not exceeding an amount
prescribed by regulation .
Section 7. The commissioner shall make available at all
times a list of fire fighting equipment , apparatus and other
fire protection items which may be purchased with allotted
moneys . No expenditures shall be made for any item not ap-
pearing on the list without first obtaining the written approval
of the commissioner .
Section 8. To carry out the purposes of this Act there
is appropriated to the Department of Public Safety out of the
General Expenditure Fund in the state treasury the sum of

( H. B. 443 )
AN ACT relating to retirement for State Police Officers .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1 . Definitions .
As used in this Act unless the context otherwise requires :
( 1 ) "System" means the State Police Retirement System
created by this Act ;
(2 ) " Board " means the Board of Trustees of the Kentucky
Employes Retirement System ;
(3) "Employer " or " State Police " means the Division of
State Police of the Department of Public Safety, or its successor ;
(4 ) "Current Service" means the number of years and
completed months of uninterrupted employment as an employe
subsequent to the effective date of this system , for which service
compensation is paid by the employer ;
( 5 ) "Prior Service" means the number of years and com-
pleted months of employment as an employe prior to the effective
date of the system , for which compensation was paid to the
employe by the Commonwealth ;
( 6) " Service" means the total of current service and prior
service ;
( 7 ) "Beneficiary " means any person designated in writing
as beneficiary for benefits payable on death of a member , signed
in such manner and on such forms as may be required by the
board , and duly filed with the board ; or it means the member's
estate if no designation of beneficiary is on file with the board ;
( 8 ) "Accumulated Contributions " at any time means the
sum of
allamounts deducted from the compensation
of

and credited to his individual account in the member's contri-
bution account
, together with interest credited on such amounts

have contributed thereto , including interest credited thereon ;
( 9) "Creditable Compensation " means the regular base
pay including pay for longevity received by an employe working
full time for the employers ; living allowances , expense reimburse-
ments , payments received after the date of termination of em-
ployment for accrued vacation leave , and other such items as are
determined by the board shall be excluded ;
( 10 ) "Final Compensation " at any time means the credit-
able compensation a member was paid during the five calendar
years he was paid the highest average monthly rate of any of the
ten years of creditable compensation preceding such time , divided
by the number of months of service during such five year period ;
such five years may be fractional , need not be consecutive , and
include only the years during which creditable compensation is
received by the member ;
( 11 ) "Retired Member " means any member entitled to
receive benefits under the provisions of this Act ;
( 12 ) "Retirement Allowance " means the retirement pay-
ments to which a retired member is entitled ;
( 13 ) "Actuarially Equivalent Benefits " means benefits
which are of equal value when computed upon the basis of such
actuarial tables as are adopted by the board ;
( 14 ) "Authorized Leave of Absence " means any time dur-
ing which a person is absent from employment but as provided
in section 8 of this Act retained in the status of an employe in
accordance with the personnel policy of the Division of State
Police ;
( 15 ) "Normal Retirement Date " means the first day of the
month following or coinciding with a member's fifty-fifth birth-
day , except that for members over age fifty-five on the effective
date of the system, it shall mean the first day of the month six
months following the effective date of the system ;
( 16 ) "Disability Retirement Date " means the first day of
the month following six complete months of total and permanent
disability or partial disability ;
( 17 ) "Dependent Child " means a child en ventre sa mere

Section 9. Contributions of Members .
( 1 ) Except for members over age fifty-five on the effective
date of this system who shall not be required to contribute , each
member shall , commencing on the date he becomes a member ,
contribute for each pay period for which he receives compen-
sation, whether or not on authorized leave of absence , ten per-
cent of his creditable compensation reduced by the Social
Security taxes paid for said pay period .
(2 ) The Commissioner of Finance shall cause to be de-
ducted from the compensation of each member for each and
every payroll period subsequent to the effective date of the
system , the contributions payable by such member as provided
in this Act .
(3 ) In determining the creditable compensation of a mem-
ber in a payroll period , the board may consider the rate of com-
pensation payable to such member on the first day of the payroll
period as continuing throughout such payroll period ; to facilitate
meeting deduction requirements , the board may allow omission
of a deduction of any fractional amount less than one dollar from
any member in any payroll period . Deductions from compensa-
tion for any period less than a full payroll period for any employe
not a member on the first day of the payroll period may be
omitted if such procedure is authorized by the board .
(4 ) Every member shall be deemed to consent to deduc-
tions made as provided herein ; and the payment of salary or
compensation less such deduction shall be a full and complete
discharge of all claims for services rendered by such person
during the period covered by such payment , except as to any
benefits provided by this Act.
(5 ) Notwithstanding any other provisions of this section ,
no deduction shall be made from any member's compensation if
the employer's contribution required hereunder is in default .
Section 10. Contributions of employer .
( 1 ) The employer shall contribute monthly , beginning
with the month of the effective date of the system . Contributions

CHAPTER 94 449
made each month shall equal an amount based on a "normal con-
tribution percentage" of the creditable compensation of the
members , to be known as the "normal contribution " and an
additional amount based on a "past service liability contribution
percentage" of such creditable compensation to be known as the
"prior service liability contribution ".
(2) Until the prior service liability contribution is no longer
required , the "normal contribution percentage" shall be deter-
mined at such rate payable during the member's periods of serv-
ice while under the system as necessary to provide the retirement
allowances due to such periods of service not provided by the
members ' contributions . After the prior service contribution is
no longer required , the " normal contribution percentage" shall
be determined as such annual rate payable during the members '
periods of service under the system as necessary to provide the
difference between the total liabilities for retirement allowances
not provided by members ' contributions and the amount of assets
in retirement allowance accounts .
( 3 ) The " prior service liability contribution percentage"
shall be such annual amount required to provide for the liquida-
tion , within thirty years of the effective date of the system , of
the liabilities for retirement allowances due to prior service with
the employer . The unfunded prior service liability at any time
shall be the excess , if any, of the then present value of the retire-
ment allowances due to prior service over the total accumulated
prior service liability contributions previously made with interest
on such contributions at the rates of interest earned each
the assets of the system .

contributions shall be determined on actuarial bases adopted by
the board . Such contributions shall be determined by the board
after each valuation and shall continue in force until a new

(5 ) The board shall furnish to the employer prior to the
beginning of each fiscal year ,
itsbest estimate
ofthe normal

for contributions and expenses .
( 6 ) The aggregate employer contributions payable into the
retirement allowance account shall be at least sufficient , when
combined with the amounts then held in the accounts , to provide
the benefits payable from such accounts during the current year.
Section 11. Assets credited to one of two accounts .
All the assets of the system shall be held and invested in the
State Police Retirement Fund and credited , according to the
purpose for which they are held , to one of two accounts : the
"member contribution account" or the "retirement allowance
account ."
Section 12. Member Contribution Account .
( 1 ) The member contribution account shall be the account
to which all members' contributions and interest allowances as
provided in this Act shall be credited ; only funds from this ac-
count shall be used to return accumulated contributions of a
member when required by reason of any provision of this Act .
(2) The Commissioner of Finance shall , at the end of each
payroll period , transfer to the member contribution account an
amount equal to the aggregate amount of the deduction made
for the preceding payroll period from the salaries of
allmembers
.
The Commissioner of Finance shall forward
arecord of all such
transfers to the board

ofthe member

ferred from the retirement allowance account
.

(6) Included as a part of each member's contribution
account shall be his accumulated contributions in the Ken-
tucky Employes ' Retirement System, if any , transferred to this
system .
Section 13. Retirement allowance account .
The retirement allowance account shall be the account in
which shall be accumulated all employer contributions , amounts
transferred from the member contribution account , and to which
all income from the investment assets of the system shall be
credited . From this account there shall be paid administrative
expenses and in addition all benefits payable under this Act.
There shall be transferred from this account to the member con-
tribution account the interest credited annually to each member's
individual accounts .
Section 14. Deposits .
For the purpose of meeting disbursements for retirement
allowances and other payments , there may be kept available cash ,
not exceeding ten percent of the total amount in both accounts
of the system , on deposit in one or more banks or trust compa-
nies that are designated by the State Treasurer as depositories
for funds of the state . The members of the board shall not be
liable for any loss sustained as to such funds that are on deposit
in such banks or trust companies.
Section 15. Minimum Benefits .
( 1 ) A person whose membership has ceased prior to the
completion of five years of service, or as a result of termination of
employment with prejudice , shall be eligible to receive a lump
sum payment equal to his accumulated contributions determined
on the date of cessation of membership .
(2 ) A person entitled to receive benefits as provided by
sections 18-26 may elect in lieu of
allsuch benefits either
ofthe
following benefits
:
(a)
Alump sum payment equal
tothe value
ofaccumu-
lated contributions determined on the date such entitled benefits
would otherwise commence
, or
(b) Monthly payments for fifteen years certain
, com-
mencing on the member's otherwise normal retirement date
,

452 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
equal on said date to the then value of his vested retirement
benefit . This provision shall not apply in event of death of a
member following termination of employment but prior to his
attainment of age fifty -five .
(3) In event of termination of disability , the difference , if
any , between accumulated contributions at the member's dis-
ability retirement date and benefits paid by reason of disability
shall be refunded to the recovered retired member .
Section 16. Disability.
(1) Total and permanent disability as used in this Act shall
mean such disability as results in a member's incapacity to engage
in any occupation for remuneration or profit , which disability is
the result of bodily injury or disease suffered or contracted in line
of duty and which has existed continuously for a period of six
months . Total disability of six month's duration is considered as
permanent only for the purpose of determining eligibility for
commencement of benefits . Loss by severance of both hands
at or above the wrists , or both feet at or above the ankle , or one
hand above the wrist and one foot at or above the ankle , or the
complete , irrecoverable loss of the sight of both eyes shall be
considered as total and permanent disability . The “disability
ratio " shall be deemed one hundred percent in the case of total
and permanent disability .
(2 ) Partial disability as used in this Act shall mean such
disability as results in a member's incapacity to continue as an
employe of the employer , and which disability has incapacitated
such member continuously for six months and is the result of
bodily injury or disease suffered or contracted in line of duty .
The board shall have full power and exclusive authority to de-
termine the existence of partial disability ; it shall establish by
appropriate findings utilizing consistent procedures , including
services of an examiner , the disability ratio applicable to such
disabled member .
( 3 ) Notwithstanding the provisions of subsections ( 1 ) and
( 2 ) , no injury shall be deemed to be disability within the pro-
visions of this Act if such injury results directly or indirectly
from :
(a ) Injury intentionally self - inflicted while sane or insane ;

CHAPTER 94 453
(b ) Injury or disease resulting from military service ; or
( c ) Disease suffered or contracted prior to date of em-
ployment as an employe of the employer .
(4 ) Upon termination of disability a member or retired
member eligible for disability benefits under this Act shall lose
all rights to such benefits that would be paid subsequent to the
termination of disability , except as provided in section 15 ( 3 ) of
this Act .
( 5 ) Once each year the board may require any retired
member receiving a disability benefit who has not reached age
fifty -five to undergo a medical examination by an examiner desig-
nated by the board , such examination to be made at a place near
the residence of such retired member or such other place as
may be mutually agreed upon . If it appears from such medical
examination that the disability of said retired member has
changed or that his earning capacity has changed since the date
disability payments commenced , the board shall revise the retired
member's "disability ratio " consistent with such findings and
disability benefits to the retired member shall be adjusted be-
ginning with the next benefit payment .
Should any member receiving a retirement allowance by
reason of disability refuse to submit to such medical examination ,
his disability benefits shall be discontinued until he shall actually
undergo such medical examination , and should he fail to undergo
such medical examination for one year , all his right in and to his
disability benefits shall cease . If a retired member has his dis-
ability benefit hereunder discontinued prior to age fifty -five and
if such member is re -employed by the employer within six months
following the date said disability ceases , then his service shall not
be deemed to have ceased or been interrupted for purposes of
determining benefits upon normal retirement .
( 6 ) Total and permanent disability shall be deemed to
cease in event a retired member receiving a benefit by reason of
total and permanent disability engages in gainful occupation or
work of any sort.
(7 ) Notwithstanding any provision of this Act to the con-
trary , no benefit provided in sections 21 or 22 shall be reduced
as a result of any change in the extent of disability or the dis-
ability ratio with respect to any retired member who is age fifty-
five or over .

454 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 17. Normal Retirement .
A member shall terminate employment on his normal re-
tirement date . Upon such retirement , the member shall receive
a monthly normal retirement allowance payable for life equal to
one and one -half percent of final compensation for each year of
service . The amount paid each month by the system will be
reduced beginning with the first payment following the sixty-fifth
birthday by eighty -five percent of the monthly primary insurance
amount under Social Security determined on the assumption that
said member would be eligible to receive the maximum primary
insurance amount determined under the Social Security law in
force at the time of his normal retirement . However , in lieu of
any benefit to commence on his normal retirement date , the
member may elect to receive a delayed allowance as provided in
section 19 of this Act .
Section 18. Vested Deferred Benefits .
Any member who has completed at least five years of service
at the time of termination of employment shall receive on his
otherwise normal retirement date a retirement allowance payable
for life computed as if it were a normal retirement allowance , but
based on service completed at time of termination of employment .
Upon death after termination of employment and prior to the
attainment of age fifty-five , such deceased member's designated
beneficiary shall be paid the then amount of the retired member's
accumulated contributions .
Section 19. Delayed Allowance .
In lieu of a retirement allowance to commence at normal
retirement date , a member , or retired member who had ter-
minated employment prior to his otherwise normal retirement
date , shall have the right , by proper written election to the board
of trustees in such manner as the board shall prescribe , to re-
ceive a monthly benefit to commence at any time subsequent
to the attainment of age fifty -five , but not later than the first
day of the month following or coinciding with the sixty -fifth
birthday . The benefit shall be payable monthly for life . The
actuarial value at normal retirement date of the retirement allow-
ance which would otherwise commence on that date shall be
determined , and shall accumulate at the rate of interest credited
to individual accounts until the payment of benefit shall com-

CHAPTER 94 455
mence , at which time the benefit to commence shall be of equiva-
lent actuarial value .
Section 20. Death in Line of Duty .
In event death of a member occurs as a direct result of an
act occuring or a thing done which , as determined by the board ,
was required in the performance of duty , the member's bene-
ficiary shall receive the following monthly benefit , computed
pursuant to the various percentages referred to as "benefit per-
centages ", applicable to the deceased member's monthly rate of
creditable compensation at time of death . The benefit shall
commence in the month following date of the death of the mem-
ber and shall be payable to the beneficiary , if living , or to a
legally appointed guardian , or the member's estate as the case
may be , as directed by the board .
( a ) For the surviving beneficiary , a "benefit percentage"
of forty -five percent shall be applicable . The resulting benefit
shall be payable until the death of the beneficiary , or until
seventy -two payments have been paid . Benefits cease , in the
case of beneficiaries who are also surviving spouses of members
upon remarriage . Notwithstanding the provisions of the section ,
if the spouse is beneficiary and remains unmarried , said benefit
shall be continued for as long a time as there is at least one de-
pendent child . Upon the death of the surviving beneficiary , if
there is then at least one dependent child , the board shall direct
that the benefit otherwise payable for the beneficiary , together
with all other income from the system by reason of dependent
children , shall be continued to a legally appointed guardian , or
such other person , persons , or organization entitled to such in-
come in the opinion of the board by reason of carrying out the
responsibilities of the orphaned dependent children .
(b ) In the period of time following a member's death dur-
ing which only one dependent child is alive , an additional "bene-

,

the resulting benefit shall be payable until remarriage
ofthe

eighteen .
(d) In the period of time following a member's death dur-
ing which three dependent children are alive , an additional
"benefit percentage" of twenty - five percent shall be applicable
and the resulting benefit shall be payable until remarriage of the
spouse or until a dependent child dies , marries , or attains age

(e) In the period of time following a member's death dur-

tional “benefit percentage " of thirty percent shall be applicable
and the resulting benefit shall be payable until remarriage of
the spouse ; or death of any dependent child , or attainment of age

Section 21. Total and Permanent Disability in Line of
Duty . In event total and permanent disability occurs as a direct
result of an act occurring or a thing done which , as determined
by the board , was required in the performance of duty , the mem-
ber shall retire on his disability retirement date and be eligible
to receive the following benefits , based on various percentages
referred to as "disability benefit percentages " which shall be ap-
plied to the member's monthly rate of creditable compensation
in effect at the time of the occurrence leading to disability . The
income shall commence in the month of the disability retirement
date :
( a ) For the disabled member, a "disability benefit percent-
age " of forty -five percent shall be applicable . The resulting
benefit shall be payable until the death of the retired member .
(b) Upon the death of the retired member , a death benefit
shall be payable to the surviving beneficiary , computed as if the
member had died in line of duty and received a benefit in accord-
ance with the provisions of section 20 of this Act .
Section 22. Partial Disability in Line of Duty .
In event partial disability occurs as a direct result of any
act occurring or a thing done which , as determined by the board ,
was required in the performance of duty , the member shall retire
on his disability retirement date and be eligible to receive a
benefit equal to the product of the " disability ratio ", as provided
in section 16 , and the amount determined according to sub-

CHAPTER 94 457
sections ( a ) and ( b ) of this section . The income shall com-
mence in the month of the disability retirement date and will be
payable to the retired member .
(a ) For the partially disabled member , forty -five percent
of the member's monthly rate of creditable compensation in effect
at the time of the occurrence leading to disability shall be pay-
able until death ;
(b ) Upon the death of the retired member , a death benefit
shall be payable to the surviving beneficiary equal to the benefits
computed as if the member had died in line of duty and received
a benefit in accordance with the provisions of section 20 of this
Act .
Section 23. Death not in Line of Duty .
In event of death of a member not a direct result of perform-
ing any act or doing anything required in the performance of
duty , the member's beneficiary shall be eligible to receive a bene-

Section 26. Death benefits after age 55.
The actuarially determined value at age fifty -five of bene-
fits otherwise payable at normal retirement date shall vest upon
a member's attainment of age fifty -five . Upon the death of a
retired member after age fifty -five but before benefit payments
begin , his beneficiary shall receive one hundred eighty monthly
installments certain .
Section 27. Optional benefits .
( 1 ) Each member shall have the right to receive an
optional allowance payable under any one of the options set forth
in this section in lieu of the retirement allowance provided in
sections 15, 17 , 18 and 19 of this Act otherwise payable to him.
The member shall make an election by written request to the
board six months prior to the time his retirement allowance or
delayed allowance would otherwise commence , and such election
must be approved by the board before payment of any benefit .
( 2 ) A beneficiary , by reason of death of a member , eligible
to receive a benefit under the provisions of this Act shall have
the right to receive an optional allowance payable under any one
of the options set forth in this section in lieu of all other benefits
provided by this Act . A beneficiary shall make an election by
written request to the board six months prior to the time any
benefits would otherwise be payable .
(3) The amount of any optional allowance determined
according to paragraphs ( a ) , ( b ) , ( c ) , and ( d ) below shall be
actuarially equivalent to the amount of retirement allowance
otherwise payable .
(a) A member may elect to receive a reduced level optional
allowance payable for life .
(b ) A member may elect to receive a decreased level
optional allowance payable for one hundred twenty payments
certain and during the lifetime of the retired member thereafter .
( c ) A member may elect to receive a decreased level
optional allowance payable during his lifetime and have such
retirement allowance continued after his death to his beneficiary
during the lifetime of such beneficiary .
(d ) A member may elect to receive a decreased level
retirement optional allowance during his lifetime and have fifty

CHAPTER 94 459
percent of such retirement allowance continued after his death
to his beneficiary during the lifetime of such beneficiary .
(4 ) The election by a member of any of the options stated
in this section shall be null and void if the member dies prior
to his normal retirement date , and the election of a member of
paragraphs ( c ) or ( d ) of subsection ( 3 ) shall be null and void
if the designated beneficiary dies , before the member's retire-
ment .
( 5 ) A member or retired member whose retirement al-
lowance has not begun , who has elected any one of the options
stated in this section , may , at any time prior to his effective
retirement date or the receipt by the board of proper written
notification of the retirement of the member , revoke such election
by proper written notification to the board .
(6) A member shall have the right to change his bene-
ficiary at any time prior to the time that benefits provided under
sections 15 , 17 , 18 and 19 would otherwise commence .
Section 28. Board to Administer System .
The government and control of the system is hereby vested
in the Board of Trustees of the Kentucky Employes ' Retirement
System . The board shall carry out the provisions of this Act in the
same manner in which it administers the Kentucky Employes '
Retirement System . In all matters concerning the administration
of this Act , and not otherwise provided for by this Act , the same
rights , duties and obligations shall apply to the board , as now
apply under the provisions of KRS 61.510 to KRS 61.700 .
Section 29. The following subjects shall be administered in
the same manner subject to the same limitations and require-
ments , as provided for the Kentucky Employes ' Retirement
System by KRS 61.675 to KRS 61.700 :
(a) Duties of the employer ;
(b ) Exemption of benefits of the system from taxation ;
(c ) Effect of action by the General Assembly with respect
to the system ;
and
(d) Effect of false statement and falsification of records ;
(e ) Penalties .

460 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 30. For the purposes of this Act there is hereby
appropriated out of the General Fund to the Department of
Public Safety the sums of $ 123,000 for the fiscal year 1958-1959
and $ 148,000 for the fiscal year 1959-1960 .
Section 31. This Act shall become effective on July 1 .
1958 .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of

CHAPTER 96
(S. B. 92 )
AN ACT relating to local public health .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 212.020 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The State Board of Health shall appoint three intelligent
and discreet licensed and practicing physicians residing in each
county who , together with the county judge and one person

CHAPTER 96 461
appointed by the fiscal court of each county , shall constitute
a local board of health for the respective counties in which they
reside . In the event a county does not have three qualified
licensed and practicing physicians residing therein , the State
Board of Health may appoint a resident lay person in lieu thereof
for each such vacancy . The members of the local board shall
hold office for a term of two years from the date of their appoint-
ment, and until their successors are appointed . The members
of the board shall receive no compensation for their services .
Section 2. Section 212.170 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Each city board of health shall appoint a health offi-
cer for the city . He may be removed at any time by the board .
( 2 ) The county board of health of each county having a
county department of health shall , subject to the approval of
the State Department of Health, appoint a health officer who
shall , subject to merit system provisions , hold office at the
pleasure of both the county board and the State Department of
Health .
(3 ) A health officer may, with the approval of the State
Department of Health and the local boards of health concerned ,
serve in such capacity for more than one county .
(4 ) A health officer may appoint an administrative assist-
ant for each county served by him subject to the approval of
the State Department of Health . An administrative assistant
shall exercise such duties as may be delegated to him by the
health officer .
(5 ) A health officer may employ and fix the compensation
of, by contract or otherwise , subject to the approval of the State
Department of Health , all medical , technical , clerical , profes-
sional and other employees necessary for the maintenance and
operation of the local health department in accordance with
standards and merit system provisions prescribed by the State
Department of Health.
(6 ) In the absence of a local health officer , the Commis-
sioner of Health or his duly appointed representative shall serve
as health officer for the county concerned .

CHAPTER 97
Approved March 31 , 1958
( S. B. 94 )
AN ACT relating to births and deaths .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 213.020 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
Each county in the state shall constitute a registration dis-
trict for the purpose of carrying out the provisions of Chapter
213 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .

CHAPTER 97 467
Section 2. Section 213.030 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Except as otherwise provided in subsection ( 3 ) of this
section, the State Department of Health shall , with the approval
of the local health officer or administrative assistant concerned ,
designate and commission an employee of each county and city-
county health department as local registrar for the county .
(2) The local registrar may designate one or more em-
ployees of the county or city -county health department as deputy
registrar . He may also appoint persons as deputy registrars who
are not employees of the local health department if, in the
opinion of the State Department of Health , such appointments
are necessary . All such appointments shall be approved by the
State Department of Health .
(3) In counties in which a county department of health
is not maintained , the State Department of Health shall appoint
a person to be the local registrar . Such person shall possess the
training and qualifications prescribed by the State Department
of Health , and shall hold office at the pleasure of the depart-
ment; provided , however , that upon the establishment of a health
department in said county , the State Department of Health shall
notify the local registrar that his employment is terminated and
order him to transfer all the records in his possession to the county
department of health .
Section 3. Section 213.050 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The attending physician or midwife shall sign and file
a certificate of birth with the local registrar of the district in
which the birth occurred within ten days after the date of birth.
The certificate shall be properly and completely filled out , and
shall contain the date of signature , the address , and all the in-
formation required by Chapter 213 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes . No physician or midwife shall refuse to file or delay
the filing of a birth certificate because obstetrical fees have not
been paid . If the mother of a child informs the physician , mid-
wife , or a person prescribed by subsection ( 2 ) of this section that
the child was born out of wedlock , the name of the father shall
not be inserted in the birth certificate and the certificate of birth
of said child shall be in the surname of the mother . However ,

468 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the Director of the Division of Vital Statistics shall insert the
name of the father and change the name of the child to the sur-
name of the father if paternity is established in a legal action , or
if an affidavit is filed stating that the affiant is the father of the
child .
(2) If there is no attending physican or midwife , the father
or mother of the child, or the head of the household , owner of
the premises , or manager or superintendent of the public or
private institution in which the birth occurred shall notify the
local registrar , within ten days after the date of birth , of the
fact of the birth .
(3 ) Upon receipt of notice of birth , as provided in sub-
section (2 ) of this section , the local registrar shall then secure
the necessary information to make a proper certificate of birth ,
and shall present it to the person who gave him notice of the
fact of birth , who shall sign the certificate .
(4 ) The certificate of birth shall be prescribed and fur-
nished by the State Department of Health and shall be designed
with due consideration for national uniformity . All certificates
shall be written legibly , in unfading ink , or typewritten . No
certificate shall be held to be complete and correct that does not
supply all items of information called for therein , or satisfactorily
account for their omission . If a certificate of birth is incomplete ,
the registrar shall immediately notify the informant , and require
him to supply the missing items if they can be obtained .
Section 4. Section 213.080 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The certificate of death shall be prescribed and fur-
nished by the State Department of Health and shall be designed
with due consideration for national uniformity . It shall be
written legibly , in unfading ink , or typewritten . The personal
and statistical particulars shall be authenticated by the signature
of the informant , who may be any competent person acquainted
with the facts . The statement of facts relating to the disposition
of the body shall be signed by the undertaker or person acting
as such.
(2) The medical certificate shall be made and signed by
the physician , if any, last in attendance on the deceased , who

CHAPTER 97 469
shall specify the time in attendance , the time he last saw the
deceased alive , and the hour of the day at which death occurred .
He shall further state the cause of death , showing the course of
disease or sequence of causes resulting in death , giving the pri-
mary cause and any contributory causes , and the duration of
each . Indefinite and unsatisfactory terms , indicating only symp-
toms of disease or conditions resulting from disease , will not
be sufficient for issuing a burial or removal permit . Any cer-
tificate not containing the terms defined by the Division of
Vital Statistics shall be returned to the physician for correction
and definition . Where death may be the result of either disease
or violence , the causes shall be carefully defined , and if the
result of violence

CHAPTER 98
( S. B. 99)
Approved March 31 , 1958
AN ACT relating to barbiturate drugs .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act :
( 1 ) The term "barbiturate " means the salts and derivatives
of Barbituric Acid also known as Malonyl Urea , having somni-
facient or hypnotic action , and compounds , preparations and
mixtures of same .
(2 ) The term " practitioner " means a person licensed under
the professional licensing laws of this state to prescribe and
administer medicines or drugs .
Section 2. ( 1 ) It shall be unlawful for any person to
possess , manufacture , sell, dispense , prescribe or administer any
barbiturate except as authorized in this Act .
(2 ) ( a ) No person shall obtain or attempt to obtain a
barbiturate , or procure or attempt to procure the administration
of a barbiturate : by fraud , deceit , misrepresentation , or subter-
fuge ; or by the forgery or alteration of a prescription ; or by the
concealment of a material fact ; or by the use of a false name
or the giving of a false address .
(b ) Information communicated to a practitioner in an
effort unlawfully to procure a barbiturate , or unlawfully to
procure the administration of such drug , shall not be deemed a
privileged communication .
(c) No person shall willfully make a false statement in or
regarding any prescription , order , report , or record required
by this Act .
(d ) No person shall , for the purpose of obtaining a bar-

CHAPTER 98 473
biturate , falsely assume the title of or represent himself to be
a manufacturer , wholesaler , pharmacist , practitioner , or other
authorized person .
(e ) No person shall make or utter any false or forged
prescription .
(f) No person shall affix any false or forged label to a
package or receptacle containing any barbiturate .
(3 ) No person shall bring into , or cause to be brought
into , or possess , use , or sell, upon the premises of any penal
institution within the Commonwealth of Kentucky , any bar-
biturate in violation of this Act .
Section 3. Duly licensed pharmacists may sell , dispense
and furnish any barbiturate only upon the prescription of a
practitioner and then only if the container of such barbiturate
bears a label showing the name and address of the pharmacy
or pharmacist , the name of the person for whom the barbiturate
was prescribed , the date of filling the prescription , and if for
an animal , the specie and owner of same . All such prescriptions
shall be numbered and filed and retained for at least two years.
No such prescription shall be refilled except upon authorization
of the prescribing practitioner .
Section 4. A practitioner may possess barbiturates in
reasonable amounts , and deliver and dispense same to his
patients in the general course of his practice provided the con-
tainer in which such barbiturates are dispensed bears a label
showing the name of the practitioner , the name of the person
for whom prescribed , date and directions for use . If prescribed
for an animal , the specie and name of the owner shall be shown
on the label . The practitioner shall keep a record of such barbi-
turates received by him, and a record of all such drugs ad-
ministered , dispensed , or professionally used by him otherwise
than by prescription .
Section 5. Wholesalers and manufacturers may possess
barbiturates for manufacturing and may sell and deliver same at
wholesale , accompanied in each case with an invoice or bill of
sale , to the following :
( 1 ) Pharmacists .
(2 ) Practitioners .

474 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 3 ) For lawful research, teaching , or testing , and not for
resale .
(4 ) Hospitals and institutions where distribution is
directed by practitioners .
( 5 ) Federal , state and local governments .
( 6 ) Manufacturers and other wholesalers .
Section 6. Nothing in this Act shall apply to a compound ,
mixture , or preparation containing salts or derivatives of bar-
bituric acid which is sold in good faith for the purpose for
which it is intended and not for the purpose of evading the
provisions of this Act if:
( 1 ) Such compound , mixture , or preparation contains a
sufficient quantity of another drug or drugs , in addition to such
salts or derivatives , to cause it to produce an action other than
its hypnotic or somnifacient action ; or
(2 ) Such compound , mixture , or preparation is intended
for use as a spray or gargle or for external application and con-
tains , in addition to such salts or derivatives , some other drug
or drugs rendering it unfit for internal administration .
Section 7. All records , prescriptions and invoices provided
for in this Act shall be preserved for at least two years and open
to inspection by authorized representatives of the State Depart-
ment of Health and the State Board of Pharmacy .
Section 8. This Act shall be administered jointly by the
State Department of Health and the State Board of Pharmacy
and their authorized agents . The State Board of Health and the
State Board of Pharmacy shall make such rules and regulations
as are deemed necessary to carry out the intent and purpose
of this Act .
Section 9. ( 1 )( 1 ) It is hereby made the duty of the State
Department of Health and the State Board of Pharmacy , their
officers , agents , inspectors , and representatives , and of all peace
officers within the state , and of all city , county , and Common-
wealth attorneys , and the Attorney General , within their respec-
tive jurisdictions , to enforce all provisions of this Act and to
cooperate with all agencies charged with the enforcement of

CHAPTER 98 475
the laws of the United States , of this state , and of all other
states relating to barbiturates .
(2 ) For the purpose of enforcing the provisions of this
Act , the investigators , inspectors , representatives , and agents of
the State Department of Health and the State Board of Pharmacy
shall have the full power and authority of peace officers in this
state , including the power of arrest and the authority to bear
arms , and shall have the power and authority to administer
oaths , to enter upon premises at all times for the purpose of
making inspections , to seize evidence , to interrogate all persons ,
to require the production of prescriptions , of books , papers ,
documents , or other evidence , to employ special investigators and
to expend funds for the purpose of obtaining evidence . Investi-
gators , inspectors , representatives and agents of the State De-
partment of Health and the State Board of Pharmacy are em-
powered to remove from the files of any apothecary or other
custodian any barbiturate prescription upon tendering a receipt
therefor .
(3 ) It shall be the duty of the State Department of Health
to make or cause to be made examinations of samples secured
under the provisions of this Act to determine whether or not
any provision thereof has been violated .
Section 10. Notwithstanding the existence or pursuit of
any other remedy ( civil or criminal ) the State Department of
Health and the State Board of Pharmacy may maintain , in their
own name, an action to restrain or enjoin any violation of this
Act .
Section 11. On the conviction of any person of a violation
of any provision of this Act , a copy of the judgment and sen-
tence , and of the opinion of the court or magistrate , if any opinion
be filed, shall be sent by the clerk of the court or by the
magistrate to the State Department of Health .
Section 12. ( 1 ) Any person who violates any provision of
subsections ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) of section 2 of this Act shall be guilty
of a felony and shall be punished by confinement in the peniten-
tiary for not less than two years nor more than five years , or
by a fine of not less than one thousand dollars nor more than
five thousand dollars or both .

476 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 2 ) Any person who violates any provision of this Act,
for which a specific penalty is not otherwise provided , shall be
punished by confinement in the county jail for one year or by
a fine of not less than three hundred dollars nor more than five
hundred dollars or both .
Section 13. Subsection ( 3 ) of section 217.010 , and sections
217.460 , 217.470 , 217.480 , 217.490 , 217.500 , 217.510 , 217.520 ,
217.530 , and subsection ( 13 ) of section 217.990 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes are repealed .
Approved March 31 , 1958

or stolen shall be forwarded to the State Department of Health as
