Section 6. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) The following property found and seized within the
Commonwealth of Kentucky is hereby declared to be contra-
band : Any vehicle , watercraft , or aircraft in which any person
is illegally possessing or transporting narcotic drugs . "Illegally
possessing " means and includes the holding of any narcotic
drugs unless lawfully acquired and intended for lawful uses .
(2 ) Any officer , agent , inspector , or representative of
the State Department of Health and any peace officer within
this state may , upon probable cause , without warrant seize
contraband as herein defined in subsection ( 1 ) of this section ,
and hold it subject to the order of the court before the owner
or one in possession of the contraband has been charged with
violation of this Act or Chapter 218 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes . Upon conviction of the defendant , the court shall enter
an order vesting title to all the contraband property in the State
Department of Health . The State Department of Health may
retain and use such property in the enforcement of this Act or
Chapter 218 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes or dispose of
such property as hereinafter provided .
(3) ( a ) Contraband property included in subsection ( 1 )
of this section shall be subject to the right of any owner or
lienor whose lien is valid and of record , to intervene and establish
his rights in the property by proving that the property was
being used in connection with traffic in narcotic drugs without
the knowledge , consent or approval of the owner or lienor . If
the owner of the property does so prove , the court shall order
the property restored to him . If the lienor so proves , the court
shall order the sale of the property at public auction , unless an
agreement is made between the lienor and the State Department
of Health which shall not become final until it has been approved
by the court . The State Department of Health may deliver any
property found to be contraband to a lienor whose claim has
been established by order of a court of competent jurisdiction ,
upon payment to the State Department of Health of the differ-
ence of the fair market value of the property so seized and the
claim of the lienor .
(b ) Where an agreement has been made between the
lienor and the State Department of Health and approved by

CHAPTER 99 481
the court , a public auction shall not be required . If an agreement
is not entered into between the State Department of Health and
the lienor or approved by the court , and a public auction is
required to be held , the public auction shall be conducted by
the sheriff of the county in which the property is seized . The
sheriff shall receive and be allowed the same fees as allowed for
sales under execution .
( c) The expenses of keeping and selling such property
and the amount of all valid recorded liens that are established
by intervention as being bona fide , shall be paid out of the
proceeds of the sales whether they are private or public . The
balance shall be deposited in the state treasury in a revolving
fund for the use of the State Department of Health in carrying
out the provisions of Chapter 218 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
(d) If the defendant is acquitted , no property seized as
contraband in connection with the arrest of the defendant shall
be ordered returned or restored unless the person from whose
possession the property was taken proves that he was in lawful
possession of the property , and if no other person appears and
proves that he owns the property or has a valid recorded lien
on the property and that the property was being used without his
knowledge and consent , title shall vest in the State Department
of Health at the end of ninety days .
( e ) If the owners or lienholders of any contraband seized
by any officer , agent , or representative of the State Department of
Health , or any peace officer of this state , cannot be located
within ninety days , and during that time fail to appear and
claim the contraband , or if the owner or lienholder appears and
agrees , title to the contraband shall immediately vest in the
State Department of Health , in which event it may sell the
contraband at a private sale or retain and use the vehicle for
the enforcement of this Act or Chapter 218 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes .
Section 7. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
No person shall bring into , or cause to be brought into , or
possess , use , or sell , upon the premises of any penal institution
within the Commonwealth of Kentucky , any narcotic drug in

482 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
violation of this Act or Chapter 218 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
Section 8. Section 218.210 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any person who sells , supplies , prescribes , adminis-
ters , dispenses or furnishes , or causes to be sold , supplied , pre-
scribed , administered , dispensed or furnished , any narcotic drug
in violation of this Act or Chapter 218 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes to a person under twenty -one years of age , shall be
guilty of a felony and shall be fined not more than twenty
thousand dollars and confined in the penitentiary for not less
than twenty years , and may be so confined for a longer period
or for life .
(2 ) (a ) Any person in possession of any narcotic drug
in violation of any provision of this Act or Chapter 218 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes , except section 218.250 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes , shall be guilty of a felony and shall ,
for the first offense be punished by confinement in the peni-
tentiary for not less than two years nor more than ten years
and by a fine of not more than twenty thousand dollars , and for
each subsequent offense be punished by confinement in the
penitentiary for not less than five years nor more than twenty
years and by a fine of not more than twenty thousand dollars .
(b ) Any person who violates any provision of this Act or
Chapter 218 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , for which a
specific penalty is not otherwise provided , except section 218.250
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , shall be guilty of a felony
and shall , for the first offense , be punished by confinement in
the penitentiary for not less than five years nor more than
twenty years and by a fine of not more than twenty thousand
dollars , and , for each subsequent offense be punished by confine-
ment in the penitentiary for not less than ten years nor more
than forty years, and by a fine of not more than twenty thousand
dollars .
(3 ) No court or judge thereof shall postpone or suspend
judgment or sentence or probate a defendant when the defend-
ant has pleaded guilty to or been convicted of selling , supplying ,
prescribing , administering , dispensing or furnishing , or causing
to be sold , supplied , prescribed , administered , dispensed or

CHAPTER 99 483
furnished , any narcotic drug in violation of this Act or Chapter
218 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes to a person under twenty-
one years of age , or when the defendant has previously pleaded
guilty to or been convicted of a violation of any of the other
provisions of this Act or Chapter 218 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , except section 218.250 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes , or has previously pleaded guilty to or been convicted of a
violation of any of the narcotic laws of the United States of
America or any state or territory thereof .
(4 ) Whenever a person charged with a violation of sub-
section ( 1 ) of section 218.250 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
waives a jury, the court or judge thereof may, upon the de-
fendant pleading or being found guilty , sentence the defendant
to imprisonment for twelve months without the intervention of a
jury . Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection ( 2 ) of sec-
tion 218.250 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , no court or judge
thereof shall postpone or suspend judgment or sentence or pro-
bate a defendant when the defendant has pleaded guilty to or
been convicted of a violation of section 218.250 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , unless the defendant consents to enter, and
forthwith enters an institution approved by the court for the
treatment of narcotic addicts and remains therein until dis-
charged by said institution ; failure of the defendant to do so
shall automatically revoke his probation and require the prompt
entry of judgment and service of sentence in the county jail .
( 5 ) Any person who violates subsection ( 2 ) of section
218.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes as amended herein ,
shall be punished by a fine of not less than one hundred dollars
nor more than five hundred dollars , and for each subsequent
offense shall be punished by imprisonment in the county jail for
ninety days and by a fine of not less than five hundred dollars .
Section 9. Section 218.250 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any person who habitually uses narcotic drugs as
defined in Section 218.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
shall be imprisoned in the workhouse or county jail for twelve
months .
(2 ) In the event a conviction is had under subsection ( 1 )
of this section in a county , quarterly , justice's or police court .

484 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
such court shall have the same authority to postpone the enter-
ing of judgment and the sentencing of the defendant , and to
place the defendant on probation , as the circuit courts have,
and the probation officers shall perform the same functions with
respect to probations made under this section as they do in the
case of probations made by the circuit court .
(3 ) Any peace officer who apprehends a person under the
influence of a narcotic drug , or who hears a person state that
he is addicted to the use of narcotics , shall immediately arrest
such person and take him before the proper court to be dealt
with according to law.
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 100
(S. B. 176 )
AN ACT relating to post - mortem examinations .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. No person shall perform an autopsy or post-
mortem examination upon the body of a deceased person except
in accordance with this Act.
Section 2. Section 72.070 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) If in the opinion of the coroner it is necessary to have
a post-mortem examination of a dead person during an inquest ,
he may employ a competent surgeon or physician for that pur-
pose , and if he suspects poisoning he may employ a competent
chemist to analyze the body or any part thereof .
( 2 ) Upon agreement of the coroner , an autopsy or post-
mortem examination may be performed upon the body of a
deceased person by a licensed physician or surgeon , whenever
written consent thereto , duly signed and acknowledged prior to
his death , has been granted by the deceased . In the absence of
such consent , written or telegraphic consent for such examination
shall be deemed sufficient when given by the person who assumes

CHAPTER 100 485
the duty of legal disposal of the body . If two or more persons
assume custody of the body , the consent of one of them shall be
deemed sufficient .
(3) No physician , surgeon or chemist shall be required to
inquire into a coroner's authority to order an autopsy to be per-
formed . Any licensed physician , surgeon or chemist who per-
forms an autopsy or post- mortem examination upon request of
the coroner or with the consent of any one of the persons enu-
merated in subsection ( 2 ) shall not be liable in damages or other-
wise accountable to any person therefor .
Section 3. Section 72.090 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) A surgeon, physician or chemist employed under KRS
72.070 ( 1 ) or 72.080 shall receive a reasonable fee fixed by the
fiscal court .
(2 ) The officer directing the investigation under KRS
72.080 shall receive per diem , as compensation for his services ,
the maximum fee prescribed by law for services required of and
rendered by him by virtue of the office he holds .
(3) The fees prescribed in subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of this
section shall be paid out of the county treasury . The other costs
of an inquest are payable out of the county treasury except
where an inquest on an unburied body is held in a city of thirty
thousand or more inhabitants , in which case the other costs of
the inquest shall be paid by the city . Provided , however , that
if there are insufficient funds available in the county treasury
for the purpose of employing the physician , surgeon or chemist
authorized to be employed by KRS 72.070 and 72.080 , the Com-
missioner of Health may , upon request of the coroner , pay for
such post- mortem examination , out of funds of the Department
of Health which are not otherwise appropriated , a sum not to
exceed one hundred dollars for each examination .
Section 4. Any person who violates section 1 or section
2 of this Act shall be fined not less than ten dollars nor more
than five hundred dollars or confined in the county jail for not
less than ten days nor more than thirty days , or both .
Approved March 31 , 1958

486 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 101
( S. B. 243 )
AN ACT relating to the Court of Appeals .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 21.080 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
Where the amount in controversy is as much as two hundred
dollars , exclusive of interest and costs , and less than twenty - five
hundred dollars , application for appeal may be made to the
Court of Appeals . The court shall promulgate rules governing
the form of the application and the procedure to be followed
after the application has been filed , which rules shall be designed
to afford more simplified procedure for determination of appeals
in cases of this class . The tax on appeals shall be paid only in
cases in which an appeal is allowed .
Approved March 28 , 1958
CHAPTER 102
(S. B. 348)
AN ACT relating to death benefits payable under the Teachers ' Retire-
ment System .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. Death benefits payable under the provisions of
Chapter 161 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes and the provi-
sions of the Teachers ' Retirement System shall be paid to bene-
ficiaries becoming eligible by May 1 , 1958 , or upon passage of
this bill and approval by the Governor , whichever date comes
first.
Section 2. WHEREAS , there is a pressing need for the
payment of such benefits immediately , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this bill becomes effective upon its passage
and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 31 , 1958

A JOINT RESOLUTION providing for an investigation and study , by
the Legislative Research Commission in cooperation with the Ken-
tucky Bankers Association , the American Bankers Association , and
interested state agencies , of the Model State Banking Code prepared
by the American Bankers Association , with particular reference to
its effect on existing Kentucky law .
WHEREAS , the proposed Model State Banking Code , as promulgated by
the American Bankers Association , is a comprehensive revision and
modernization of the field of banking law ; and
WHEREAS , several states are studying the effects on their existing bank-
ing law the Model State Banking Code would have if enacted , and
the desirability of enacting the Code into law ; and
WHEREAS , an opportunity should be given to interested groups and
citizens as well as to members of the banking community to express
their opinions , either in support of or in opposition to the Model State
Banking Code ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. That the Legislative Research Commission is
authorized and directed to make an investigation and study of
the proposed Model State Banking Code , to determine
itseffect
on existing Kentucky law
, and
tocollect such information con-
cerning the Code

tobe
advised fully
ofthe factors bearing on the advisability and
desirability
ofenacting the Code into law
.

ofbankers

, and public
-minded citizens

mission
.

and the American Bankers Association
.

ofFinance
, the State Treasurer
, and such other state agencies
asthe Legislative Research Commission may designate

Research Commission in the preparation of the report .
Section 5. That the Legislative Research Commission shall
include
itsfindings

ofthe
Commonwealth
ofKentucky

the 1960 Regular Session
.

CHAPTER 107 495
CHAPTER 106
(H. B. 216 )
AN ACT relating to the support of indigent persons .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 387.281 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
The circuit and county courts shall have jurisdiction within
their respective counties , without impanelling a jury , to appoint
a special committee to receive funds from any state , federal ,
city or county agency , which disburses funds for the maintenance
and support of indigent persons , for any person who is a resident
of their respective counties , or for any person who is confined
in any of the charitable institutions in their respective counties ,
except that no such special committee shall be appointed under
this section to receive funds disbursed by the Commonwealth
through the Division of Public Assistance or by the United States
through the Veterans Administration , or the successors of those
agencies .
CHAPTER 107
Approved March 31 , 1958
AN ACT relating to stockyards .
(H. B. 265 )
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
When the owner or operator of any licensed stockyards in
this state shall be required by law or shall have any reason to
utilize the services of a veterinarian , the said veterinarian shall
be chosen by the owner or operator of the stockyards , and may
be relieved of his duties at the pleasure of the owner or operator .
Any veterinarian so employed shall be properly qualified and
licensed to do business in this state .
Approved March 31 , 1958

496 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 108
(H. B. 319 )
AN ACT relating to the exercise of the right of eminent domain by com-
panies authorized to construct or operate a railroad .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Any company authorized to construct or operate a railroad
may, at its option , condemn land or material necessary for its
use in the manner provided by KRS 416.230 to 416.310 , in lieu of
following the procedure set out in KRS 416.010 to 416.080 .
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 109
(H. B. 412 )
AN ACT relating to utility gross receipts taxes .
Section 136.240 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
(1) Subject to the exceptions contained in subsection (2)
of this section , and except as provided in KRS 279.200 , an excise
tax is hereby imposed on the sale of electric current , water , arti-
ficial , natural or mixed gas , and on furnishing the ways or means
for the transmission of the voice or of messages between persons
in this state , other than those transmitted by United States Mail ,
equal to three percent of the aggregate gross receipts of the per-
sons engaged in such business . Every such person shall collect
the tax from the purchaser and account to the state for the tax
in the manner provided by KRS 136.250 .
(2) No tax is imposed upon the transmission of the voice
or messages for, or upon sales of electric current , water , arti-
ficial , natural or mixed gas to the United States , this state , any
city or other political subdivision of this state , or any charitable ,
religious or educational institution in this state . No tax is im-
posed upon sales of electric current , water , artificial , natural or
mixed gas for the purpose of resale , nor upon the sale of electric
current or artificial or natural gas for use in manufacturing ,

CHAPTER 111 497
processing , refining or for use in producing , extracting , or mining
coal , petroleum , natural gas or mineral deposits . Sales of elec-
tric current , water , artificial , natural or mixed gas in interstate
commerce are specifically excluded from the provisions of this
section .
Approved March 31 , 1958
[ The enrolled copy of this bill has no enacting clause .]
CHAPTER 110
( H. B. 426 )
AN ACT relating to the operation of school busses .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 189.375 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
Section 1. No school bus shall be licensed or operated for
the transportation of school children unless it has a folding sign
on the driver's side thereof , with letters at least six inches in
height and displaying the word " Stop" on both sides . Upon
stopping the school bus the driver shall open out the sign so that
it will be plainly visible to traffic approaching from both direc-
tions . No driver shall stop a school bus for loading or unloading
purposes in a no passing area , such as a hill or curve , which
does not afford reasonable visibility to approaching motor ve-
hicles from both directions .
Section 2. Any person who violates any provision of this
Act shall be fined not less than ten nor more than one hundred
dollars .
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 111
AN ACT relating to parole .
(H. B. 447 )
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 345 of the Criminal Code of Practice is amended to
read as follows :
If the defendant , upon a new trial , be again convicted , the
period of his former confinement in the penitentiary shall be

498 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
allowed the prisoner by the Division of Corrections of the De-
partment of Welfare , in determining the prisoner's eligibility
date for parole on the new sentence and release date by expira-
tion of sentence .
CHAPTER 112
Approved March 31 , 1958
(H. B. 448 )
AN ACT relating to prison traffic .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Any person who delivers , conspires with others to deliver ,
or attempts to deliver any article or thing to any prisoner con-
fined in a state penal institution , or who shall receive any
article or thing from any prisoner and conveys or attempts to con-
vey it out of said institution , contrary to the rules and regulations
thereof , shall be imprisoned for not less than one year nor
more than five years.
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 113
(H. B. 466 )
AN ACT relating to the Kentucky Employees ' Retirement System , and
extending coverage to employees of the Department of Military
Affairs .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 61.510 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
As used in KRS 61.510 to 61.700 , unless the context other-
wise requires :
( 1 ) “ System ” means the Kentucky Employees ' Retirement
System created by KRS 61.510 to 61.700 ;
( 2 ) "Board " means the Board of Trustees of the system as
provided in KRS 61.645 ;
(3 ) "Department " means any state department , board or
agency participating in the system in accordance with appro-
priate executive order , as provided in KRS 61.520 ;

CHAPTER 113 499
( 4 ) “Examiner " means the medical examiner or examiners
as provided in KRS 61.665 ;
( 5 ) “ Employee " means every person regularly employed
in rendering service to and for a department and whose com-
pensation is payable in whole or in part by such department .
The term shall include all full -time , regular employees of the
board and employees of the Department of Military Affairs
whose compensation is paid wholly or in part by the federal
government . In case of any doubt , the board shall determine if a
person is an employee within the meaning of KRS 61.510 to
61,700 ;
(6 ) "Employer " means a department or any authority of
such department having the power to appoint an employee to
office or employment in such department ;
( 7 ) "State " means the Commonwealth of Kentucky ;
(8 ) "Member " means any employee who is included in
the membership of the system ;
( 9) "Service " means the total of current service and prior
service as defined herein ;
( 10 ) " Current service" means the number of years and
completed months , expressed as a fraction of a year , of employ-
ment as an employee , on and after July 1 , 1956 , for which com-
pensation is paid by the employer during which employment
the employee is a member of the system , except as otherwise
provided in KRS 61.620 ;
( 11 ) "Prior service" means the number of years and com-
pleted months , expressed as a fraction of a year , of employment
as an employee , prior to July 1 , 1956 , for which compensation
was paid by the employer .
( 12 ) "Beneficiary " means any person entitled to receive
benefits under KRS 61.510 to 61.700 ;
( 13 ) "Accumulated contributions " at any time means the
sum of all amounts deducted from the compensation of a mem-
ber and credited to his individual account in the members'
contribution account , together with interest credited on such
amounts, and any other amounts the member shall have con-
tributed thereto , including interest credited thereon ;
( 14 ) "Creditable compensation " means the compensation
to an employee ( arising from funds disbursed by the State

500 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Treasurer or , with respect to employees of the Department of
Military Affairs , arising from funds disbursed either by the State
Treasurer or by the federal government ) working the full work-
ing time for his position . In cases where compensation includes
maintenance and other perquisites , the board shall fix the value
of that part of the compensation not paid in money ;
( 15 ) "Final compensation" of a member means the cred-
itable compensation to the member during the five calendar
years he was paid at the highest average monthly rate of any
of the last ten years of creditable compensation preceding the
member's retirement date divided by the number of months of
service during that five -year period . Said five years may be
fractional , need not be consecutive , and include only years dur-
ing which creditable compensation is received by the member .
In no case will final compensation be less than that determined
on the assumption that the member retired on his normal retire-
ment date . All benefits shall be paid in accordance herewith .
( 16 ) "Retirement allowance " means the retirement pay-
ments to which a member is entitled ;
( 17 ) "Actuarial equivalent ” means a benefit of equal value
when computed upon the basis of such actuarial tables as are
from time to time adopted by the board ;
( 18 ) "Normal retirement date" means the sixty-fifth birth-
day of a member or the date on which the member completes ten
years of service , whichever occurs later , except that in no case
will a member's normal retirement date be later than his seventy-
fifth birthday . The normal retirement date for any member who
has passed his seventy - fifth birthday on the date he becomes a
member shall be the date he becomes a member , except as other-
wise provided in KRS 61.590 . Notwithstanding anything to the
contrary contained in this subsection ( 18 ) , the normal retire-
ment date of a member shall not be earlier than July 1 , 1957 ;
( 19 ) "Fiscal year" of the system means the twelve months
from July 1 through the following June 30 .
Section 2. Section 61.560 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) ( a ) Each member shall , commencing on the date
he becomes a member , contribute for each pay period for which
he receives compensation two and one -half percent of his cred-

CHAPTER 113 501
itable compensation if his rate of annual compensation does not
exceed $ 4,200 . If such member's rate of annual compensation
exceeds $4,200 , he shall contribute two and one-half percent
of that portion of his creditable compensation not in excess of
the equivalent applicable rate for such pay period to annual
compensation of $ 4,200 and four percent of that portion of his
creditable compensation which is in excess of such rate applicable
to annual compensation of $ 4,200 .
(b ) Each employee of a department on the date the
department first participates in the system who elects to become
a member subsequent to such date , but prior to the next suc-
ceeding October 1 , shall make contributions which he would
have made if he had elected to become a member as of the date
such department first participated .
( 2 ) The Commissioner of Finance shall cause to be de-
ducted from the salary or compensation of each member whose
compensation is paid by the state , for each and every payroll
period subsequent to the date of establishment of the system ,
and the Adjutant General shall collect from each member in
the Department of Military Affairs , whose compensation is paid
wholly or in part by the federal government at the time of pay-
ment , the contribution payable by each such member as provided
in KRS 61.510 to 61.700 , but in no case shall any deduction be
made from the compensation of a member after his normal retire-
ment date if such member elects not to contribute .
(3) In determining the creditable compensation of a mem-
ber in a payroll period , the board may consider the rate of com-
pensation payable to such member on the first day of the payroll
period as continuing throughout such payroll period , and , to
facilitate the making of deductions , may allow the omission of
deductions from any fractional amount less than one dollar from
any member in any period and may allow the omission of de-
ductions from compensation for any period less than a full pay-
roll period if any employee was not a member on the first day
of the payroll period .
( 4 ) The deductions provided for herein shall be made
notwithstanding that the minimum compensation provided by
law for any member shall be reduced thereby . Every member
shall be deemed to consent and agree to the deductions made
as provided herein ; and payment of salary or compensation less

502 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
such deductions shall be a full and complete discharge of all
claims for services rendered by such person during the period
covered by such payment , except as to any benefits provided
by KRS 61.510 to 61.700 .
( 5 ) Notwithstanding any other provisions of this section ,
no deduction shall be made from any member's compensation
if the employer's contribution required hereunder is in default .
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 114
(H. B. 498 )
AN ACT relating to indigent tuberculosis patients .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) Every citizen and resident of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky who has been discharged from a tuberculosis hospital
within the Commonwealth of Kentucky who is recommended
for continued drug therapy by the hospital director , and who ,
upon a showing to the commissioner of the State Board of
Health that he or she cannot pay for such drug therapy , shall
be granted a certificate by the State Board of Health which will
entitle said person to secure all drugs needed for the treatment
of tuberculosis without charge to such person .
( 2 ) The cost of such drugs shall be paid by the State
Board of Health upon presentment of the verified bill by the
person dispensing said drugs .
Approved March 28 , 1958
CHAPTER 115
(H. B. 605 )
AN ACT relating to public lakes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
( 1 ) The Department of Highways is authorized to con-
struct fills , made necessary by road construction , so that such
will impound water .

CHAPTER 116 503
(2 ) The Department of Fish and Wildlife and the De-
partment of Highways shall decide whether the creation of a
lake in any one particular spot is deemed reasonable and bene-
ficial . The Department of Fish and Wildlife shall do every-
thing necessary to insure good fishing and may regulate boating
and other recreational interest so that there may be the least
amount of interference .
(3 ) The Department of Fish and Wildlife may , if the
site is suitable , assist the local party or parties in obtaining
deeds or easements where necessary . Interested local parties
may obtain title to excess land required and deed it to the
Commonwealth for creation of a public fishing lake . A parking
lot at or near the fill may be created on land deeded free of
charge to the Commonwealth .
(4) Surrounding landowners may by permission irrigate
from said lake providing that there is no adverse affect on the
lake itself .
(5) Parking shall be regulated to prevent obstruction of
the highway .
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 116
( H. R. 15 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION making an appropriation out of the General Ex-
penditure Fund of the state treasury for the purpose of meeting de-
ficiencies in the 1956-57 foundation program allotments to school
districts .
WHEREAS , in 1956 , at the time of the final calculation of foundation
program allotments to local school districts for 1956-57 , it was dis-
covered that the appropriation for said fiscal year was insufficient
to finance the total program including the guarantee provisions of
KRS 157.400 ( 2 ) ; and
WHEREAS , said deficiency was caused by an unexpected increase in the
number and training of personnel employed in local school districts ;
and
WHEREAS , because of said deficiency in funds , certain school districts
were given reduced allotments during said fiscal period ;
NOW, THEREFORE ,

504 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. There is hereby appropriated to the State De-
partment of Education , Public School Foundation Fund , the sum
of $ 171,301.01 to be used to pay the aforesaid deficiencies in the
1956-57 allotments to school districts .
Section 2. Whereas , there is an immediate need for money
to be made available for the payment of the already outstanding
claims of certain school districts which were forced to receive
reduced allotments during the aforesaid fiscal year , an emergency
is declared to exist and this resolution shall take effect upon its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 117
( H. R. 16 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing and directing payment of the state's
share of increased jailer's fees covering the period June 17 , 1954 , to
June 30 , 1957 , inclusive , and making an appropriation therefor .
WHEREAS , 1954 c 120 increased the fee that jailers received from the
state treasury from 60c to 75c for imprisoning and releasing a prisoner
charged with a felony or contempt ; and
WHEREAS , 1956 c 95 , sec . 2 increased the fee that jailers received from
the state treasury from $ 2.00 to $ 6.00 for each day's attendance upon
the circuit court and from $ 1.25 to $ 1.75 per day for keeping and
dieting a prisoner charged with a felony or contempt ; and
WHEREAS , through inadvertence , the General Assembly in 1954 and
again in 1956 did not appropriate any money to pay the aforesaid
increased fees ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. There is hereby appropriated to the county
costs account the sum of $ 100,000 to be used to pay the state's
share of the aforesaid increased fees covering the period June
17 , 1954 to June 30 , 1957 , inclusive . The jailer in each county
so affected shall certify to the Department of Finance his sup-
plemental claim covering the increased fees due him on claims
which have been properly allowed and certified by the circuit
court . The supplemental claim shall be made according to

CHAPTER 119 505
the term of court in which the original claim was allowed .
Upon such proper certification , the Department of Finance
shall audit each claim and , if found correct , authorize its
payment .
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 118
(H. R. 17 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing and directing the payment of cer-
tain increased jailer's fees for fiscal year 1957-58 , and making an
appropriation therefor .
WHEREAS , 1956 c 95 , sec . 2 increased the jailer's fee payable from the
state treasury from $ 2.00 to $ 6.00 for each day's attendance upon the
circuit court and from $ 1.25 to $ 1.75 per day for keeping and dieting
a prisoner charged with a felony or contempt ; and
WHEREAS , through inadvertence , the 1956 General Assembly did not
appropriate any money to pay the increases in jailer's fees ; and
WHEREAS , notwithstanding the lack of an appropriation therefor , the
increased fee schedule was put into effect on July 1, 1957 ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. There is hereby appropriated to the county
costs account the sum of $ 100,000.00 to be used to pay the
state's share of increased jailer's fees authorized by 1956 c 95,
sec . 2, during the 1957-58 fiscal year .
Section 2. Whereas , there is an immediate need for money
to be made available in order to prevent a total deficiency of
funds in the county costs account in the fourth quarter of the
1957-58 fiscal year , an emergency is hereby declared to exist
and this resolution shall take effect upon its passage and ap-
proval by the Governor .
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 119
(H. R. 18 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing reimbursement to C. S. Woodward
for funds expended in payment of premium on official bond .

506 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , it appears that C. S. Woodward was during the period of
January 4 , 1954 to January 4 , 1956 the duly elected and acting sheriff
of Robertson county ;
WHEREAS , it appears C. S. Woodward as such duly elected and acting
sheriff of Robertson county did , as required by law , cause to be exe-
cuted an official bond by an incorporated surety company for each of
the indicated years ;
WHEREAS , it appears that the said C. S. Woodward paid the premiums
in the amount of one hundred and thirty-five dollars on the said
bonds for the years indicated ;
WHEREAS , by the provisions of section 62.155 o fthe Kentucky Revised
Statutes each sheriff has a valid claim against the state for the
amounts paid by him for premiums on the bonds required of him by
law when the bonds have been executed by an incorporated surety
company authorized to do a surety business in Kentucky , and when
the claims are verified by the sheriff's affidavit ; and
WHEREAS , the said C. S. Woodward has made a properly verified claim
against the Department of Finance of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky in the amount of one hundred and thirty -five dollars , the total
amount of premiums paid by the said C. S. Woodward on the official
bonds required of him for the years indicated , which claim the said
Department of Finance has refused to pay for the reason that said
claim was not presented for payment within the time prescribed by
section 45.231 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. That C. S. Woodward has a proper claim against
the Commonwealth in the amount of one hundred and thirty -five
dollars for premiums paid by the said C. S. Woodward on of-
ficial bonds which he , the said C. S. Woodward , as the duly
elected and acting sheriff of Robertson county during the period
January 4 , 1954 to January 4 , 1956 , was required by law to have
executed .
Section 2. That, it appearing that said claims have not
been paid , the Commissioner of Finance is hereby authorized
and directed to draw a warrant in favor of C. S. Woodward ,
when the claim is presented , in the amount aforesaid and trans-
mit same to the State Treasurer , who shall issue a check in favor
of the said C. S. Woodward in the amount of one hundred and
thirty-five dollars .
Approved March 31 , 1958

CHAPTER 121 507
CHAPTER 120
(S. B. 51 )
AN ACT relating to attorneys at law.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. Any veteran of World War II or the Korean
conflict who was a resident of Kentucky at time of entry into
service , who has been graduated with an LLB , from a law school
approved by the American Bar Association or by the American As-
sociation of Law Schools , shall be permitted to take the Kentucky
Bar Examination , and upon passage shall be granted a license to
practice law by the Clerk of the Court of Appeals , provided he
was enrolled or did enroll in any such approved law school dur-
ing the years 1941 through 1945 or 1950 through 1952 , and was
subsequently graduated therefrom .
Section 2. Whereas it is important to the veterans and the
people of the Commonwealth that this measure take effect be-
fore the February bar examination , an emergency is declared to
exist and this Act shall become effective upon
itsapproval by
the Governor
.
Approved March
31, 1958

CHAPTER 121 513
It shall file with the Commissioner of Insurance :
( a ) a certified copy of the written contract containing in
full the terms and conditions of the consolidation or merger ;
(b ) a sworn statement by the president and secretary or
corresponding officers of each society showing the financial
condition thereof on a date fixed by the Commissioner of In-
surance but not earlier than December thirty -first , next preceding
the date of the contract ;
(c ) a certificate of such officers , duly verified by their
respective oaths , that the consolidation or merger has been
approved by a two -thirds vote of the supreme legislative or
governing body of each society ; and
(d ) evidence that at least sixty days prior to the action
of the supreme legislative or governing body of each society ,
the text of the contract has been furnished to all members of
each society either by mail or by publication in full in the
official organ of each society .
If the Commissioner of Insurance finds that the contract
is in conformity with the provisions of this section , that the
financial statements are correct and that the consolidation or
merger is just and equitable to the members of each society, he
shall approve the contract and issue his certificate to such effect .
Upon such approval , the contract shall be in full force and
effect unless any society which is a party to the contract is
incorporated under the laws of any other state or territory . In
such event the consolidation or merger shall not become effective
unless and until it has been approved as provided by the laws
of such state or territory and a certificate of such approval filed
with the Commissioner of Insurance of this state or , if the laws
of such state or territory contain no such provision , then the
consolidation or merger shall not become effective unless and
until it has been approved by the Commissioner of Insurance of
such state or territory and a certificate of such approval filed
with the Commissioner of Insurance of this state .
Upon the consolidation or merger becoming effective as
herein provided , all the rights , franchises and interests of the
consolidated or merged societies in and to every species of
property , real , personal or mixed , and things in action thereunto
belonging shall be vested in the society resulting from or re-
maining after the consolidation or merger without any other

514 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
instrument , except that conveyances of real property may be
evidenced by proper deeds , and the title to any real estate or
interest therein , vested under the laws of this state in any of
the societies consolidated or merged , shall not revert or be in
any way impaired by reason of the consolidation or merger but
shall vest absolutely in the society resulting from or remaining
after such consolidation or merger .
The affidavit of any officer of the society or of anyone
authorized by it to mail any notice or document , stating that
such notice or document has been duly addressed and mailed ,
shall be prima facie evidence that such notice or document has
been furnished the addressees .

Section 18. The member shall have the right at all times
to change the beneficiary or beneficiaries in accordance with
the constitution , laws or rules of the society . Every society by
its constitution , laws or rules may limit the scope of beneficiaries
and shall provide that no beneficiary shall have or obtain any
vested interest in the proceeds of any certificate until the
certificate has become due and payable in conformity with the
provisions of the insurance contract .
A society may make provision for the payment of funeral
benefits to the extent of such portion of any payment under
a certificate as might reasonably appear to be due to any
person equitably entitled thereto by reason of having incurred
expense occasioned by the burial of the member , provided the
portion so paid shall not exceed the sum of $ 500 .
Any society may pay the benefits due to minors in monthly
instalments of not over twenty -five dollars per month for each
minor beneficiary to the person or persons who in the opinion
of the society are caring for and supporting such beneficiary ,
and such payment shall be a complete discharge of the society's
liability to the extent of such payments .
If, at the death of any member , there is no lawful bene-
ficiary to whom the insurance benefits shall be payable , the
amount of such benefits , except to the extent that funeral
benefits may be paid as hereinbefore provided , shall be pay-
able to the personal representative of the deceased member .
Section 19. No money or other benefit , charity , relief or
aid to be paid , provided or rendered by any society , shall be
liable to attachment , garnishment or other process , or to be
seized , taken, appropriated or applied by any legal or equitable
process or operation of law to pay any debt or liability of a
member or beneficiary , or any other person who may have a
right thereunder , either before or after payment by the society.

Section 22. No domestic , foreign or alien society author-
ized to do business in this state shall issue or deliver in this
state any certificate or other evidence of any contract of acci-
dent insurance or health insurance or of any total and permanent
disability insurance contract unless and until the form thereof ,
together with the form of application and all riders or en-
dorsements for use in connection therewith , shall have been
filed with the Commissioner of Insurance and approved by him
as conforming to reasonable rules and regulations from time
to time made by him and as not inconsistent with any other
provisions of law applicable thereto . The Commissioner of In-
surance shall , within a reasonable time after the filing of any
such form , notify the society filing the same either of his
approval or of his disapproval of such form . The Commissioner
of Insurance may approve any such form which in his opinion
contains provisions on any one or more of the several require-
ments made by him which are more favorable to the members
than the one or ones so required . Pursuant to the foregoing
provisions the Commissioner of Insurance shall have power ,
from time to time , to make , alter and supercede reasonable
regulations prescribing the required , optional and prohibited
provisions in such contracts , and such regulations shall conform ,

sections , he may prescribe the portions or summary thereof of

ber . Any filing made hereunder shall be deemed approved
unless disapproved within sixty days from the date of such
filing.

Section 39. No person shall cause or permit to be made ,
issued or circulated in any form :
(a ) any misrepresentation or false or misleading state-
ment concerning the terms , benefits or advantages of any fra-
ternal insurance contract now issued or to be issued in this
state , or the financial condition of any society ;
(b ) any false or misleading estimate or statement con-
cerning the dividends or shares of surplus paid or to be paid by
any society on any insurance contract ; or
( c) any incomplete comparison of an insurance contract
of one society with an insurance contract of another society or
insurer for the purpose of inducing the lapse , forfeiture or sur-
render of any insurance contract . A comparison of insurance
contracts is incomplete if it does not compare in detail :
(1 ) the gross rates , and the gross rates less any dividend
or other reduction allowed at the date of the comparison ; and
(2 ) any increase in cash values , and all the benefits
provided by each contract for the possible duration thereof as
determined by the life expectancy of the insured ;
or if it omits from consideration :
(3 ) any benefits or value provided in the contract ;
(4 ) any differences as to amounts or period of rates ;
or
( 5 ) any differences in limitations or conditions or pro-
visions which directly or indirectly affect the benefits . In any
determination of the incompleteness or misleading character of
any comparison or statement , it shall be presumed that the
insured had no knowledge of any of the contents of the contract
involved .
Any person who violates any provision of this section or
knowingly receives any compensation or commission by or in
consequence of such violation , shall upon conviction be punished
by a fine of not less than one hundred dollars nor more than five
hundred dollars or by imprisonment in the county jail not less
than thirty days nor more than one year , or both fine and
imprisonment and shall in addition , be liable for a civil penalty
in the amount of three times the sum received by such violator
as compensation or commission , which penalty may be sued
for and recovered by any person or society aggrieved for his

540 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or its own use and benefit in accordance with the provisions
of civil practice .
Section 40. No society doing business in this state shall
make or permit any unfair discrimination between insured and
members of the same class and equal expectation of life in the
premiums charged for certificates of insurance , in the dividends
or other benefits payable thereon or in any other of the terms and
conditions of the contracts it makes .
No society, by itself , or any other party , and no agent or
solicitor , personally , or by any other party , shall offer , promise ,
allow, give , set off, or pay , directly or indirectly , any valuable
consideration or inducement to , or for insurance , on any risk au-
thorized to be taken by such society , which is not specified in the
certificate . No member shall receive or accept, directly or in-
directly , any rebate of premium , or part thereof , or agent's or
solicitor's commission thereon , payable on any certificate or re-
ceive or accept any favor or advantage or share in the dividends
or other benefits to accrue on , or any valuable consideration or
inducement not specified in the contract of insurance .

(c ) domestic societies which limit their membership to em-
ployees of a particular city or town , designated firm , business
house or corporation which provide for a death benefit of not
more than four hundred dollars or disability benefits of not more
than three hundred fifty dollars to any person in any one year,
or both ; or
(d ) domestic societies or associations of a purely religious ,
charitable or benevolent description , which provide for a death
benefit of not more than four hundred dollars or for disability
benefits of not more than three hundred fifty dollars to any one
person in any one year , or both.
Any such society or association described in clauses ( c ) or
(d ) supra which provides for death or disability benefits for
which benefit certificates are issued , and any such society or
association included in paragraph ( d ) which has more than one
thousand members , shall not be exempted from the provisions of
the Act but shall comply with all requirements thereof .
No society which, by the provisions of this section , is exempt
from the requirements of this Act , except any society described
in paragraph (b ) , supra , shall give or allow , or promise to give or
allow to any person any compensation for procuring new mem-
bers .
Every society which provides for benefits in case of death or
disability resulting solely from accident , and which does not ob-
ligate itself to pay natural death or sick benefits shall have all
of the privileges and be subject to all the applicable provisions
and regulations of this Act except that the provisions thereof re-
lating to medical examination , valuations of benefit certificates ,
and incontestability , shall not apply to such society.
The Commissioner of Insurance may require from any society
or association , by examination or otherwise , such information as
will enable him to determine whether such society or association
is exempt from the provisions of this Act .
Section 44. Any person who wilfully makes a false or fraud-
ulent statement in or relating to an application for membership
or for the purpose of obtaining money from or a benefit in any
society , shall upon conviction be fined not less than $ 100 nor more

542 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
than $ 500 or imprisonment in the county jail not less than thirty
days nor more than one year , or both .
Any person who wilfully makes a false or fraudulent state-
ment in any verified report or declaration under oath required or
authorized by this Act, or of any material fact or thing contained
in a sworn statement concerning the death or disability of a mem-
ber for the purpose of procuring payment of a benefit named in
the certificate , shall be guilty of perjury and shall be subject to
the penalties therefor prescribed by law.
Any person who solicits membership for , or in any manner
assists in procuring membership in , any society not licensed to
do business in this state shall upon conviction be fined not less
than $ 50 nor more than $200 .
Any person guilty of a wilful violation of , or neglect or re-
fusal to comply with , the provisions of this Act for which a
penalty is not otherwise prescribed , shall upon conviction , be
subject to a fine of not more than $ 200 .
Section 45. If any provision of this Act or the application
of such provision to any circumstances is held invalid , the re-
mainder of the Act or the application of the provision to other
circumstances , shall not be affected thereby .
Section 46. Chapter 300 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is repealed .

CHAPTER 122

AN ACT relating to the Kentucky Geological Survey .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 151.010 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
There is hereby established and shall be maintained within
the University of Kentucky a bureau of geological research and
information to be known and designated as the Kentucky Geo-
logical Survey . The survey shall be under the direction of a
person who shall be designated director of the survey and who

of the University . The survey shall have a non -teaching staff
to be appointed in the same manner as other staff members of
the University . The director or a member of the non -teaching
staff shall be designated State Geologist and all members of the
survey shall be employes of the University of Kentucky within
the provisions of KRS 164.220 to 164.230 , inclusive .

data
asare

ofthe survey

, maps and other publications for sale

ofthe University

138.565 shall be paid out of the Road Fund . Separate suits

violation shall be served on the person by the attendance officer ,
and one day shall be given for the termination of the violation .

of KRS 159.010 to 159.170 are again violated during the school
term by the child , no further notice shall be necessary and the
parent or guardian shall be punishable as provided in KRS
159.990 . A notice by registered mail or by personal service by
the attendance officer shall be a legal notice .

Section 45. This Act is subordinate to all other Acts of the
1958 General Assembly enacted before or after the passage of
this Act . In case of any conflict between a provision of this Act
and a provision of any other Act adopted at this session , the
provisions of the other Act shall prevail .
Section 46. The Legislative Research Commission shall
analyze the statutes of the Commonwealth and prepare a revi-
sion thereof , classifying , consolidating and re -arranging the
sections for purposes of classification , brevity and simplicity and
removing conflicts , implied repeals and other errors resulting

CHAPTER 127 563
from new legislation and sectional revision . In this revision
the Commission shall not alter the sense or effect of the statutes
in any way . The general review directed by this Act shall be
in addition to the regular statute revision work being performed
by the Commission . The Commission may proceed by either
topical revision or bulk revision and shall submit a report of the
general revision program to the 1960 Session of the General
Assembly . Such report shall include such revised statutes as
are recommended for enactment by the General Assembly .
Approved March 31 , 1958

Section 3. A new section of Chapter 281 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is hereby created to read as follows :
As used in this chapter , unless the context requires otherwise :
( 1 ) "Suburban area " means territory wholly within the
State of Kentucky which extends not more than two air miles
beyond the corporate limits of any municipality and one mile ad-
ditional for each fifty thousand population or major fraction
thereof , and , where one or more municipalities are contiguous or

joining municipalities shall be considered in determining the
suburban area of each such contiguous or adjoining municipality ;
provided , however , the department may, from time to time, upon
a showing as hereinafter provided , authorize and permit the
holder of a suburban bus certificate to extend its operations be-
yond the territorial limits of any such city or cities for a distance
not to exceed ten air miles , if said department finds :
(a) That the territory proposed to be served adjoins or is
contiguous to said city ; and
(b ) That the territory proposed to be served has such
density of population and that the physical , business , economic
and social interest is such as to make it an integral part of the
city to which it adjoins or is contiguous and that it would be in
the public interest and to the benefit of persons residing in said
territory that they be served by the holder of a suburban bus
certificate ;

Section 6. Section 281.605 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby repealed and re -enacted to read as follows :
The provisions of this chapter shall not apply , except as
to the safety regulations , to :
(1) Motor vehicles used as school buses and while en-
gaged in the transportation of students , under the supervision
and control and at the direction of school authorities ;
(2) Motor vehicles , regardless of ownership , used exclu-
sively :
( a ) For the transportation of agricultural and dairy prod-
ucts, including fruit, livestock , meats , fertilizer , wood , lumber ,
cotton , products of grove or orchard , poultry and eggs , while
owned by the producer of the products , including landlord
where the relation of landlord and tenant or landlord and crop-
per is involved , from the farm to a market , warehouse , dairy or
mill , or from one market , warehouse , dairy or mill to another
market , warehouse , dairy or mill ; or
(b ) For the transportation of agricultural and dairy prod-
ucts , livestock , farm machinery , feed , fertlizer , and other
materials and supplies essential to farm operation , from market
or shipping terminal to farm ; or
(c ) For both the purposes described in subparagraphs ( a )
and (b ) of this paragraph ; or
(d ) For the transportation of agricultural and dairy prod-
ucts from farm to regularly organized fairs and exhibits and
return ;
(3) Motor vehicles used exclusively as church buses and
while operated in the transportation of persons to and from a
church or place of worship or for other religious work under
the supervision and control and at the direction of church au-
thorities ;
(4) Motor vehicles used exclusively in transporting ma-
terials , supplies and equipment for the construction , reconstruc-
tion or maintenance of any public highways , road or street in
this state ;
(5) Motor vehicles used exclusively for the transportation
of property belonging to a nonprofit cooperative association or
its members where the vehicle is owned or leased exclusively
by the association ;

CHAPTER 130 573
(6) Motor vehicles owned in whole or in part by any per-
son and used by such person to transport petroleum products
and those incidental supplies used or sold in connection with
the wholesale or retail sale of such petroleum products of which
petroleum products or incidental supplies , such person is the
owner , lessee , consignee , or bailee ; provided , however , that such
transportation is for the purpose of sale , lease , rent or bailment ,
and is an incidental adjunct to an established private business
owned and operated by such person in the furtherance of any
commercial enterprise other than the business of transportation
of property for hire .
Section 7. A new section of Chapter 281 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is hereby created to read as follows :
The following vehicles operated by a carrier or motor carrier
shall be exempt from the tax imposed by KRS 281.810 and KRS
281.813 ; motor vehicles registered under subsection (3 ) of KRS
186.050 or motor vehicles owned by any person not a resi-
dent of this state and duly registered under the laws of any other
state , which when operated in this state are used exclusively
for the transportation of property for hire , or as a U-Drive -It ,
within the corporate limits of the city named in the affidavit
hereinafter required to be filed or within ten miles of the limits
of such city if such city is a city of the first , second , third or
fourth class , or within five miles of the limits of such city if
such city is a city of the fifth or sixth class . Operation outside
of this state shall not be considered in determining whether or
not this exemption applies . When claiming exemption under this
section, the carrier shall file with the department an affidavit
stating that the motor vehicle when used in this state is to be
used only for the transportation of property within the city
named in the affidavit and the area above set out , and that the
vehicle will not be used outside of such city and the area above
set out during the current registration period . The carrier shall
furnish such other information as may be required by the rules
and regulations of the department , and shall otherwise be sub-
ject to the provisions of KRS Chapter 281 and the rules and
regulations of the Department of Motor Transportation .
Section 8. Section 281.617 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :

nothing herein shall be so construed as to permit the holder of
a common carrier certificate authorizing operations over regular
routes to pick up and deliver freight having

Section 13. Section 281.675 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Every rate , fare and charge demanded or received by
any common carrier or irregular route common carrier shall be
just and reasonable, and every such carrier shall furnish adequate ,
efficient , safe and reasonable service .
( 2 ) Every contract made by a contract carrier for trans-
portation service shall be just and reasonable , and shall be com-
parable to the rate charged by any common carrier , and such
contract carrier shall furnish adequate, efficient , safe and rea-
sonable service .
Section 14. Section 281.680 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Under rules and regulations prescribed by the depart-
ment , every common carrier or irregular route common carrier
shall maintain on file with the department a schedule of rates ,
fares , charges and classifications , and a time schedule , if any, of
all motor vehicles operated under a certificate , and shall keep
open for public inspection at designated offices so much of such
schedules as the department deems necessary for public infor-
mation .
( 2 ) Under rules and regulations prescribed by the depart-
ment , every contract carrier shall maintain on file with the de-
partment all contracts made for transportation , and shall keep
open for public inspection at designated offices such contracts
as the department deems necessary for public information .
(3 ) No schedule of rates need be filed for the transpor-
tation of any commodity that has been exempted by the depart-
ment , except that the department may require the filing of a
schedule of rates .
(4) The department shall have full power concerning the
control of rates and contracts under
itsrules and regulations
.

quires otherwise :

employment reasonably permit ; and

594 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 136
(H. B. 283 )
AN ACT relating to the title to school property .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 162.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
The title to all property owned by a school district is vested
in the Commonwealth for the benefit of the district board of edu-
cation . In the acquisition of land for school purposes , whether
by purchase or condemnation , or otherwise , the title obtained
shall be in fee simple , except that title to land received from
the federal government or any agency thereof can be received
in other than fee simple with the approval of the Attorney Gen-
eral of the Commonwealth . Any reversionary interest in any
land held by boards of education on June 14 , 1934 , shall not de-
prive such boards of the ownership of the buildings or other im-
provements thereon .
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 137
(H. B. 312)
AN ACT relating to insurance and amending section 56.180 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 56.180 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
If at the end of any fiscal year the moneys and securities
to the credit of the State Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund ex-
ceed two million dollars ( $ 2,000,000 ) , any such excess shall
be available to the Department of Finance for the purpose of
improving conditions relating to life and fire safety in state
properties . Such improvements may include but not be limited
to the constructing and equipping of a fire house in the Capital
City . Allocations by the Department of Finance shall be made
on the basis of recommendations made pursuant to KRS 56.170
by the Commissioner of Insurance .
Approved March 31 , 1958

CHAPTER 139 595
CHAPTER 138
(H. B. 353 )
AN ACT relating to the transportation of mental patients .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 202.190 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) When the superintendent of any institution receives
the notice provided for by KRS 202.180 , he shall immediately
send a competent attendant or shall designate a competent per-
son from the county in which the person has been adjudged in-
sane to convey the person to the institution , and only the actual
traveling expenses of conveying any person to the institution shall
be paid . Each female committed to any institution shall be ac-
companied by a female attendant , unless accompanied by her
father , brother , husband or son . No more than one person shall
be compensated from the state treasury for conveying a patient .
(2 ) Itemized statement of such expenses shall be approved
by the superintendent and forwarded to the Commissioner of
Mental Health , who shall present the statement to the Depart-
ment of Finance . Expenses shall be paid in the same manner as
that prescribed for the payment of claims . Expenses incurred in
returning patients to counties from which they were sent shall be
paid in the same manner as expenses incurred in conveying pa-
tients to institutions .
Approved March 31 , 1958
CHAPTER 139
(H. B. 377)

WHEREAS , the timberlands of the Commonwealth , consisting of 11 ,-
500,000 acres out of a total area of 25,513,000 acres , with forest acre-
age in every county of the Commonwealth ranging from two to
ninety - two percent , constitute one of its most valuable resources ,
furnishing raw products to approximately eleven hundred plants
and mills in the state employing eighteen thousand persons with an
annual payroll of $ 37,000,000, providing protection and habitat to
wildlife resources and contributing more than any other factor to
the success of water conservation and flood control programs ;

596 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , under the present law only forty - seven counties, represent-
ing 6,873,000 , or approximately fifty - nine percent of the total timber-
alnd acreage , are participating in and receiving protection under
the state voluntary cooperative fire protection plan ; and under such
law , during the last five years , 18,695 fires have occurred in non-
participating counties as against 7,865 fires in participating counties ;
WHEREAS , Kentucky ranks fourth in the states of the Union in number
of individual timberland owners , there being 243,488 of such owners
in the Commonwealth , and is the only state in region seven of the
United States forest service consisting of fourteen states that does
not have a state - wide forest fire control law ; and
WHEREAS , forest fire prevention , detection and suppression is the con-
cern of every county in the state and is necessary if the natural re-
sources and general economy of the state is to be maintained ;
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. As used in this Act, unless the context requires
otherwise :
( 1 ) "Director " means the Director of the Division of
Forestry .
(2 ) "Division " means the Division of Forestry .
(3 ) "Timberland " means any land which has enough timber
or woody brush , standing or down , to constitute a fire menace
to itself or adjoining lands , but does not include lands under
cultivation or entirely in grass , nor land that is an isolated fire
risk unless a fire on it would imperil the lands of an adjoining
landowner .
Section 2. ( 1 ) It shall be the duty of the division to
establish and maintain a state -wide system of forest -fire preven-
tion , detection and suppression .
(2 ) The division shall formulate a plan and program subject
to the approval of the Commissioner of Conservation to extend
forest fire protection to
allcounties not now participating
inthe
state program
. The plan shall provide for the establishment
of
such protection
inall the counties
ofthe state on
orbefore
January
1,1964
.
Section
3.
(
1
) Each county receiving state forest fire
protection on the effective date
ofthis Act shall repay into the
division until January
1, 1959
, any amounts expended
inthe

CHAPTER 139 597
proceeding month by the division in such county for forest fire
protection , not exceeding in any one year an amount equivalent
to two cents per acre of privately owned timberlands in the
county , according to the United States Forest Reappraisal Study
of 1945 subject to additions and deductions of acreage since
the making of such study .
(2 ) When facilities and trained personnel are available the
director may give written notice to the county judge of any
county not now participating in the state protection program
that such services are available for establishment . Such notice
shall be given at least sixty days before January 1 of the year
in which the program is to be established .
Section 4. ( 1 ) After January 1 , 1959 , all counties receiving
state forest fire protection and all counties having received notice
of the intended establishment of such service shall annually pay
to the division a sum equivalent to two cents per acre of privately
owned timberlands according to the United States Forest Reap-
praisal Study of 1948-1952 , subject to additions and deductions
of acreage since the making of such study . Such payments shall
be due and payable on September 15 following the assessment .
(2 ) Contributions may be met out of the general funds of
the county and in addition the fiscal court may require each
owner of timberland in the county to participate directly by
paying into a special county forest fire protection fund an
assessment not exceeding three cents per year for each acre of
timberland of which he is the owner .
( 3 ) In any case in which the division and the governing
body of any county cannot agree upon the additions or
deductions to privately owned forest acreage in a particular
county , or to changes in forest acreage from year to year , the
question shall be submitted to the judge of the circuit court
of the county by a summary and informal proceeding , in term
or vacation , and a decision of the judge , certified to the govern-
ing body and to the division respectively , shall be conclusive
and final.
Section 5. ( 1 ) If the county governing body requires the
owners to participate , it shall determine the amount of the
assessment for each acre , and shall order the assessment to

penalties provided in KRS 134.020 for state , county and

Section 9. A separate fund shall be maintained by the
county treasurer to be known as the county forest fire protection
fund . The assessments collected from each timberland owner ,
together with penalties , shall constitute the fund and it shall be
used only for making payments to the division in accordance
with provisions of section 4 hereof .
Section 10. ( 1 ) Any person aggrieved by a listing of any
portion of his property as timberland by the county tax com-
missioner may file a protest with the county judge , provided the
protest is in writing and is made within thirty days of receipt
of notice of the listing . Protest may be made on the ground
that the land or any portion so listed is not timberland , or that
the timberland so listed will not be benefited by the forest fire
protective system then in effect , or benefited by the proposed
forest fire protective system if the assessment is being made for
the first time in that county .
(2 ) The county judge shall hear all protests properly filed .
Protests shall be heard within a reasonable time after the filing
thereof , and reasonable notice shall be given to the protestant
and the director of the division as to the time and place of the
hearing . The county judge shall have authority to issue sub-
poenas to compel the attendance of any witness desired by any
interested party , and he shall be authorized to administer the
oath to any witness .
( 3 ) Proceedings for hearing any protest shall be summary
and the findings of the county judge shall be final, and an order
shall be entered either dismissing the protest or directing the
proper county officials to alter their records in accordance with
the findings .
Section 11. Sections 149.100 , 149.110 , 149.120 , 149.135 ,
149.140 , 149.150 , 149.151 , 149.152 , 149.153 , 149.154 , 149.155 ,
149.156 , 149.157 , and 149.190 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
are repealed .
Approved March 31 , 1958

600 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 140
(H. B. 442 )
AN ACT relating to state lands and buildings .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 56.500 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
No state agency, except as provided in this Chapter , shall
have power or authority :
( 1) To acquire by purchase , lease , condemnation or other
means involving the expenditure of money , any real estate , except
that any state agency having the power under other laws to
acquire by purchase , lease , condemnation or other means may
continue to exercise that power subject to the approval of the
Commissioner of Finance ;
( 2 ) To lease , sell or convey any real estate belonging to
the state or to any state agency , except that any state agency
having the power under other laws to lease , sell or convey real
estate devoted to the exclusive use of such agency may continue
to exercise that power subject to the approval of the Commis-
sioner of Finance .
Section 2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
In any case where the real estate that is proposed to be sold ,
pursuant to KRS 56.463 , is in the custody of a state agency , the
department may submit to such agency a general description of
the intended use of the land , along with such detailed informa-
tion as the agency may require , but the finding of the department
on the question of selling and conveying the real estate shall
be final .
Section 3. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
When the department finds that buildings and improve-
ments are needed on land occupied by the State Fair and Exposi-
tion Center to promote the purposes of the State Fair Board , it
may, in addition to the other methods prescribed by law, au-
thorize the construction of such buildings and improvements by

CHAPTER 141 601
private capital under a lease -back agreement consistent with such
purposes .
To accomplish such a plan , the department may convey to a
private corporation or individual subject to a contract to con-
struct such buildings and improvements and to reconvey the
land , buildings and improvements to the state encumbered by a
mortgage to secure a portion of the construction costs , provided
the mortgage shall not constitute a general obligation or debt of
the Commonwealth and that in the event of default , the Com-
monwealth may, if it so elects , redeem the mortgage . Any such
authorization shall be made only with the consent of any state
agency occupying or using the land and shall be in the name of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and shall be signed by the Com-
missioner of Finance and the Governor and lodged for record
in the office of the Secretary of State .
Approved March 31 , 1958
